<!-- Generator: Widdershins v4.0.1 -->

<h1 id="data-api">Data API v1.0.0</h1>

> Scroll down for code samples, example requests and responses. Select a language for code samples from the tabs above or the mobile navigation menu.

The Data API provides web3 application developers with multi-chain data related to Avalanche's primary network, Avalanche subnets, and Ethereum. With Data API, you can easily build products that leverage real-time and historical transaction and transfer history, native and token balances, and various types of token metadata. The API is in Beta and may be subject to change.</br></br>If you have feedback or feature requests for the API, please submit them <a href="https://portal.productboard.com/dndv9ahlkdfye4opdm8ksafi/tabs/4-glacier-api">here</a>. Bug reports can be submitted <a href="https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeJQrcp7QoNiqozMDKrVJGX5wpU827d3cVTgF8qa7t_J1Pb-g/viewform">here</a>, and any potential security issues can be reported <a href="https://immunefi.com/bounty/avalabs">here</a>.

Base URLs:

* <a href="https://data-api.avax.network">https://data-api.avax.network</a>

# Authentication

* API Key (apiKey)
    - Parameter Name: **x-glacier-api-key**, in: header. Api keys provide higher access to rate limits. To obtain an api key, sign up for an account at  https://build.avax.network/console/utilities/data-api-keys.

<h1 id="data-api-evm-chains">EVM Chains</h1>

Find information about indexed EVM-compatible chains, such as the Avalanche C-Chain.

## listAddressChains

<a id="opIdlistAddressChains"></a>

> Code samples

```shell
# You can also use wget
curl -X GET https://data-api.avax.network/v1/address/{address}/chains \
  -H 'Accept: application/json' \
  -H 'x-glacier-api-key: API_KEY'

```

```http
GET https://data-api.avax.network/v1/address/{address}/chains HTTP/1.1
Host: data-api.avax.network
Accept: application/json

```

```javascript

const headers = {
  'Accept':'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key':'API_KEY'
};

fetch('https://data-api.avax.network/v1/address/{address}/chains',
{
  method: 'GET',

  headers: headers
})
.then(function(res) {
    return res.json();
}).then(function(body) {
    console.log(body);
});

```

```ruby
require 'rest-client'
require 'json'

headers = {
  'Accept' => 'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key' => 'API_KEY'
}

result = RestClient.get 'https://data-api.avax.network/v1/address/{address}/chains',
  params: {
  }, headers: headers

p JSON.parse(result)

```

```python
import requests
headers = {
  'Accept': 'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key': 'API_KEY'
}

r = requests.get('https://data-api.avax.network/v1/address/{address}/chains', headers = headers)

print(r.json())

```

```php
<?php

require 'vendor/autoload.php';

$headers = array(
    'Accept' => 'application/json',
    'x-glacier-api-key' => 'API_KEY',
);

$client = new \GuzzleHttp\Client();

// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();

try {
    $response = $client->request('GET','https://data-api.avax.network/v1/address/{address}/chains', array(
        'headers' => $headers,
        'json' => $request_body,
       )
    );
    print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
 }
 catch (\GuzzleHttp\Exception\BadResponseException $e) {
    // handle exception or api errors.
    print_r($e->getMessage());
 }

 // ...

```

```java
URL obj = new URL("https://data-api.avax.network/v1/address/{address}/chains");
HttpURLConnection con = (HttpURLConnection) obj.openConnection();
con.setRequestMethod("GET");
int responseCode = con.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(
    new InputStreamReader(con.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
    response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

```

```go
package main

import (
       "bytes"
       "net/http"
)

func main() {

    headers := map[string][]string{
        "Accept": []string{"application/json"},
        "x-glacier-api-key": []string{"API_KEY"},
    }

    data := bytes.NewBuffer([]byte{jsonReq})
    req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", "https://data-api.avax.network/v1/address/{address}/chains", data)
    req.Header = headers

    client := &http.Client{}
    resp, err := client.Do(req)
    // ...
}

```

`GET /v1/address/{address}/chains`

*List all chains associated with a given address*

Lists the chains where the specified address has participated in transactions or ERC token transfers, either as a sender or receiver. The data is refreshed every 15 minutes.

<h3 id="listaddresschains-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|address|path|string|true|A wallet address.|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "indexedChains": [
    {
      "chainId": "string",
      "status": "OK",
      "chainName": "string",
      "description": "string",
      "platformChainId": "string",
      "subnetId": "string",
      "vmId": "string",
      "vmName": "EVM",
      "explorerUrl": "string",
      "rpcUrl": "string",
      "wsUrl": "string",
      "isTestnet": true,
      "utilityAddresses": {
        "multicall": "string"
      },
      "networkToken": {
        "name": "Wrapped AVAX",
        "symbol": "WAVAX",
        "decimals": 18,
        "logoUri": "https://images.ctfassets.net/gcj8jwzm6086/5VHupNKwnDYJvqMENeV7iJ/fdd6326b7a82c8388e4ee9d4be7062d4/avalanche-avax-logo.svg",
        "description": "string"
      },
      "chainLogoUri": "string",
      "private": true,
      "enabledFeatures": [
        "nftIndexing"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "unindexedChains": [
    "string"
  ]
}
```

<h3 id="listaddresschains-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[ListAddressChainsResponse](#schemalistaddresschainsresponse)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<aside class="warning">
To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None
</aside>

`GET /v1/chains`

*List chains*

Lists the AvaCloud supported EVM-compatible chains. Filterable by network.

<h3 id="supportedchains-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|network|query|[Network](#schemanetwork)|false|Either mainnet or testnet/fuji.|
|feature|query|[ApiFeature](#schemaapifeature)|false|Filter by feature.|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|network|mainnet|
|network|fuji|
|network|testnet|
|feature|nftIndexing|
|feature|webhooks|
|feature|teleporter|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "chains": [
    {
      "chainId": "string",
      "status": "OK",
      "chainName": "string",
      "description": "string",
      "platformChainId": "string",
      "subnetId": "string",
      "vmId": "string",
      "vmName": "EVM",
      "explorerUrl": "string",
      "rpcUrl": "string",
      "wsUrl": "string",
      "isTestnet": true,
      "utilityAddresses": {
        "multicall": "string"
      },
      "networkToken": {
        "name": "Wrapped AVAX",
        "symbol": "WAVAX",
        "decimals": 18,
        "logoUri": "https://images.ctfassets.net/gcj8jwzm6086/5VHupNKwnDYJvqMENeV7iJ/fdd6326b7a82c8388e4ee9d4be7062d4/avalanche-avax-logo.svg",
        "description": "string"
      },
      "chainLogoUri": "string",
      "private": true,
      "enabledFeatures": [
        "nftIndexing"
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

<h3 id="supportedchains-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[ListChainsResponse](#schemalistchainsresponse)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

`GET /v1/chains/{chainId}`

*Get chain information*

Gets chain information for the EVM-compatible chain if supported by AvaCloud.

<h3 id="getchaininfo-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|chainId|path|string|true|A supported evm chain id or blockchain id. Use the `/chains` endpoint to get a list of supported chain ids.|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "chainId": "string",
  "status": "OK",
  "chainName": "string",
  "description": "string",
  "platformChainId": "string",
  "subnetId": "string",
  "vmId": "string",
  "vmName": "EVM",
  "explorerUrl": "string",
  "rpcUrl": "string",
  "wsUrl": "string",
  "isTestnet": true,
  "utilityAddresses": {
    "multicall": "string"
  },
  "networkToken": {
    "name": "Wrapped AVAX",
    "symbol": "WAVAX",
    "decimals": 18,
    "logoUri": "https://images.ctfassets.net/gcj8jwzm6086/5VHupNKwnDYJvqMENeV7iJ/fdd6326b7a82c8388e4ee9d4be7062d4/avalanche-avax-logo.svg",
    "description": "string"
  },
  "chainLogoUri": "string",
  "private": true,
  "enabledFeatures": [
    "nftIndexing"
  ]
}
```

|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted,

    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|

```php
    print_r($e->getMessage());
 }

 // ...
```


`GET /v1/chains/address/{address}`

**[Deprecated]** Gets a list of all chains where the address was either a sender or receiver in a transaction or ERC transfer. The list is currently updated every 15 minutes.

⚠️ **This operation will be removed in a future release.  Please use /v1/address/:address/chains endpoint instead** .

<h3 id="getaddresschains-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|address|path|string|true|A wallet address.|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "indexedChains": [
    {
      "chainId": "string",
      "status": "OK",
      "chainName": "string",
      "description": "string",
      "platformChainId": "string",
      "subnetId": "string",
      "vmId": "string",
      "vmName": "EVM",
      "explorerUrl": "string",
      "rpcUrl": "string",
      "wsUrl": "string",
      "isTestnet": true,
      "utilityAddresses": {
        "multicall": "string"
      },
      "networkToken": {
        "name": "Wrapped AVAX",
        "symbol": "WAVAX",
        "decimals": 18,
        "logoUri": "https://images.ctfassets.net/gcj8jwzm6086/5VHupNKwnDYJvqMENeV7iJ/fdd6326b7a82c8388e4ee9d4be7062d4/avalanche-avax-logo.svg",
        "description": "string"
      },
      "chainLogoUri": "string",
      "private": true,
      "enabledFeatures": [
        "nftIndexing"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "unindexedChains": [
    "string"
  ]
}
```

<h3 id="getaddresschains-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[ListAddressChainsResponse](#schemalistaddresschainsresponse)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<aside class="warning">
To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None
</aside>

`GET /v1/chains/allTransactions`

**[Deprecated]** Lists the latest transactions for all supported EVM chains. Filterable by status.

⚠️ **This operation will be removed in a future release.  Please use /v1/transactions endpoint instead** .

<h3 id="listalllatesttransactions-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 100.|
|network|query|[Network](#schemanetwork)|false|Either mainnet or testnet/fuji.|
|status|query|[TransactionStatus](#schematransactionstatus)|false|A status filter for listed transactions.|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|network|mainnet|
|network|fuji|
|network|testnet|
|status|failed|
|status|success|

> Example responses

`GET /v1/blocks`

*List latest blocks across all supported EVM chains*

Lists the most recent blocks from all supported EVM-compatible chains. The results can be filtered by network.

<h3 id="listlatestblocksallchains-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 100.|
|network|query|[Network](#schemanetwork)|false|Either mainnet or testnet/fuji.|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|network|mainnet|
|network|fuji|
|network|testnet|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "nextPageToken": "string",
  "blocks": [
    {
      "chainId": "43114",
      "blockNumber": "339",
      "blockTimestamp": 1648672486,
      "blockTimestampMilliseconds": 1648672486000,
      "blockMinDelayExcess": 7477290,
      "blockHash": "0x17533aeb5193378b9ff441d61728e7a2ebaf10f61fd5310759451627dfca2e7c",
      "txCount": 0,
      "baseFee": "string",
      "gasUsed": "string",
      "gasLimit": "string",
      "gasCost": "string",
      "parentHash": "string",
      "feesSpent": "string",
      "cumulativeTransactions": "string"
    }
  ]
}
```

<h3 id="listlatestblocksallchains-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[ListEvmBlocksResponse](#schemalistevmblocksresponse)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<aside class="warning">
To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None
</aside>

```javascript

  'x-glacier-api-key':'API_KEY'
};
```

```php
<?php

require 'vendor/autoload.php';

$headers = array(
    'Accept' => 'application/json',
    'x-glacier-api-key' => 'API_KEY',
);

$client = new \GuzzleHttp\Client();

// Define array of request body.

try {
    $response = $client->request('GET','https://data-api.avax.network/v1/chains/{chainId}/blocks', array(
```

```java
}
in.close();
```

```go
)
```

`GET /v1/chains/{chainId}/blocks`

*List latest blocks*

Lists the latest indexed blocks on the EVM-compatible chain sorted in descending order by block timestamp.

<h3 id="getlatestblocks-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 100.|
|chainId|path|string|true|A supported evm chain id or blockchain id. Use the `/chains` endpoint to get a list of supported chain ids.|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "nextPageToken": "string",
  "blocks": [
    {
      "chainId": "43114",
      "blockNumber": "339",
      "blockTimestamp": 1648672486,
      "blockTimestampMilliseconds": 1648672486000,
      "blockMinDelayExcess": 7477290,
      "blockHash": "0x17533aeb5193378b9ff441d61728e7a2ebaf10f61fd5310759451627dfca2e7c",
      "txCount": 0,
      "baseFee": "string",
      "gasUsed": "string",
      "gasLimit": "string",
      "gasCost": "string",
      "parentHash": "string",
      "feesSpent": "string",
      "cumulativeTransactions": "string"
    }
  ]
}
```

<h3 id="getlatestblocks-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[ListEvmBlocksResponse](#schemalistevmblocksresponse)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<aside class="warning">
To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None
</aside>

`GET /v1/chains/{chainId}/blocks/{blockId}`

*Get block*

Gets the details of an individual block on the EVM-compatible chain.

<h3 id="getblock-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|chainId|path|string|true|A supported evm chain id or blockchain id. Use the `/chains` endpoint to get a list of supported chain ids.|
|blockId|path|string|true|A block identifier which is either a block number or the block hash.|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "chainId": "43114",
  "blockNumber": "339",
  "blockTimestamp": 1648672486,
  "blockTimestampMilliseconds": 1648672486000,
  "blockMinDelayExcess": 7477290,
  "blockHash": "0x17533aeb5193378b9ff441d61728e7a2ebaf10f61fd5310759451627dfca2e7c",
  "txCount": 0,
  "baseFee": "string",
  "gasUsed": "string",
  "gasLimit": "string",
  "gasCost": "string",
  "parentHash": "string",
  "feesSpent": "string",
  "cumulativeTransactions": "string"
}
```

|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is

<h1 id="data-api-evm-transactions">EVM Transactions</h1>

Find information about transactions on EVM-compatible chains, such as the Avalanche C-Chain.

`GET /v1/transactions`

*List the latest transactions across all supported EVM chains*

Lists the most recent transactions from all supported EVM-compatible chains. The results can be filtered based on transaction status.

<h3 id="listlatesttransactionsallchains-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 100.|
|network|query|[Network](#schemanetwork)|false|Either mainnet or testnet/fuji.|
|status|query|[TransactionStatus](#schematransactionstatus)|false|A status filter for listed transactions.|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|network|mainnet|
|network|fuji|
|network|testnet|
|status|failed|
|status|success|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "nextPageToken": "string",
  "transactions": [
    {
      "blockNumber": "339",
      "blockTimestamp": 1648672486,
      "blockTimestampMilliseconds": 1648672486000,
      "blockMinDelayExcess": 7477290,
      "blockHash": "0x17533aeb5193378b9ff441d61728e7a2ebaf10f61fd5310759451627dfca2e7c",
      "chainId": "43114",
      "blockIndex": 0,
      "txHash": "0x3e9303f81be00b4af28515dab7b914bf3dbff209ea10e7071fa24d4af0a112d4",
      "txStatus": "1",
      "txType": 1,
      "gasLimit": "51373",
      "gasUsed": "51373",
      "gasPrice": "470000000000",
      "nonce": "1",
      "from": {
        "name": "Wrapped AVAX",
        "symbol": "WAVAX",
        "decimals": 18,
        "logoUri": "https://images.ctfassets.net/gcj8jwzm6086/5VHupNKwnDYJvqMENeV7iJ/fdd6326b7a82c8388e4ee9d4be7062d4/avalanche-avax-logo.svg",
        "address": "0x71C7656EC7ab88b098defB751B7401B5f6d8976F"
      },
      "to": {
        "name": "Wrapped AVAX",
        "symbol": "WAVAX",
        "decimals": 18,
        "logoUri": "https://images.ctfassets.net/gcj8jwzm6086/5VHupNKwnDYJvqMENeV7iJ/fdd6326b7a82c8388e4ee9d4be7062d4/avalanche-avax-logo.svg",
        "address": "0x71C7656EC7ab88b098defB751B7401B5f6d8976F"
      },
      "method": {
        "callType": "NATIVE_TRANSFER",
        "methodHash": "0xa9059cbb",
        "methodName": "transfer(address,uint256)"
      },
      "value": "10000000000000000000"
    }
  ]
}
```

<h3 id="listlatesttransactionsallchains-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[ListNativeTransactionsResponse](#schemalistnativetransactionsresponse)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<a id="opIdgetDeploymentTransaction"></a>

> Code samples

```shell
# You can also use wget
curl -X GET https://data-api.avax.network/v1/chains/{chainId}/contracts/{address}/transactions:getDeployment \
  -H 'Accept: application/json' \
  -H 'x-glacier-api-key: API_KEY'

```

```http
GET https://data-api.avax.network/v1/chains/{chainId}/contracts/{address}/transactions:getDeployment HTTP/1.1
Host: data-api.avax.network
Accept: application/json

```

```javascript

const headers = {
  'Accept':'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key':'API_KEY'
};

fetch('https://data-api.avax.network/v1/chains/{chainId}/contracts/{address}/transactions:getDeployment',
{
  method: 'GET',

  headers: headers
})
.then(function(res) {
    return res.json();
}).then(function(body) {
    console.log(body);
});

```

```ruby
require 'rest-client'
require 'json'

headers = {
  'Accept' => 'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key' => 'API_KEY'
}

result = RestClient.get 'https://data-api.avax.network/v1/chains/{chainId}/contracts/{address}/transactions:getDeployment',
  params: {
  }, headers: headers

p JSON.parse(result)

```

```python
import requests
headers = {
  'Accept': 'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key': 'API_KEY'
}

r = requests.get('https://data-api.avax.network/v1/chains/{chainId}/contracts/{address}/transactions:getDeployment', headers = headers)

print(r.json())

```

```php
<?php

require 'vendor/autoload.php';

$headers = array(
    'Accept' => 'application/json',
    'x-glacier-api-key' => 'API_KEY',
);

$client = new \GuzzleHttp\Client();

// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();

try {
    $response = $client->request('GET','https://data-api.avax.network/v1/chains/{chainId}/contracts/{address}/transactions:getDeployment', array(
        'headers' => $headers,
        'json' => $request_body,
       )
    );
    print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
 }
 catch (\GuzzleHttp\Exception\BadResponseException $e) {
    // handle exception or api errors.
    print_r($e->getMessage());
 }

 // ...

```

```java
URL obj = new URL("https://data-api.avax.network/v1/chains/{chainId}/contracts/{address}/transactions:getDeployment");
HttpURLConnection con = (HttpURLConnection) obj.openConnection();
con.setRequestMethod("GET");
int responseCode = con.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(
    new InputStreamReader(con.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
    response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

```

```go
package main

import (
       "bytes"
       "net/http"
)

func main() {

    headers := map[string][]string{
        "Accept": []string{"application/json"},
        "x-glacier-api-key": []string{"API_KEY"},
    }

    data := bytes.NewBuffer([]byte{jsonReq})
    req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", "https://data-api.avax.network/v1/chains/{chainId}/contracts/{address}/transactions:getDeployment", data)
    req.Header = headers

    client := &http.Client{}
    resp, err := client.Do(req)
    // ...
}

```

`GET /v1/chains/{chainId}/contracts/{address}/transactions:getDeployment`

*Get deployment transaction*

If the address is a smart contract, returns the transaction in which it was deployed.

<h3 id="getdeploymenttransaction-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|chainId|path|string|true|A supported evm chain id or blockchain id. Use the `/chains` endpoint to get a list of supported chain ids.|
|address|path|string|true|Contract address on the relevant chain.|
|currency|query|[CurrencyCode](#schemacurrencycode)|false|The currency that return values should use. Defaults to USD.|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|currency|usd|
|currency|eur|
|currency|aud|
|currency|cad|
|currency|chf|
|currency|clp|
|currency|cny|
|currency|czk|
|currency|dkk|
|currency|gbp|
|currency|hkd|
|currency|huf|
|currency|jpy|
|currency|nzd|

<h3 id="getdeploymenttransaction-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[GetTransactionResponse](#schemagettransactionresponse)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<aside class="warning">
To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None
</aside>

## listContractDeployments

<a id="opIdlistContractDeployments"></a>

> Code samples

```shell
# You can also use wget
curl -X GET https://data-api.avax.network/v1/chains/{chainId}/contracts/{address}/deployments \
  -H 'Accept: application/json' \
  -H 'x-glacier-api-key: API_KEY'

```

```http
Accept: application/json
```

`GET /v1/chains/{chainId}/contracts/{address}/deployments`

*List deployed contracts*

Lists all contracts deployed by the given address.

<h3 id="listcontractdeployments-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 100.|
|chainId|path|string|true|A supported evm chain id or blockchain id. Use the `/chains` endpoint to get a list of supported chain ids.|
|address|path|string|true|A wallet address.|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "nextPageToken": "string",
  "contracts": [
    {
      "name": "Wrapped AVAX",
      "description": "string",
      "officialSite": "string",
      "email": "string",
      "logoAsset": {
        "assetId": "string",
        "imageUri": "string"
      },
      "bannerAsset": {
        "assetId": "string",
        "imageUri": "string"
      },
      "color": "string",
      "resourceLinks": [
        {
          "type": "Blog",
          "url": "string"
        }
      ],
      "tags": [
        "string"
      ],
      "address": "0x71C7656EC7ab88b098defB751B7401B5f6d8976F",
      "deploymentDetails": {
        "txHash": "string",
        "deployerAddress": "string",
        "deployerContractAddress": "string"
      },
      "ercType": "ERC-721",
      "symbol": "WAVAX"
    }
  ]
}
```

<h3 id="listcontractdeployments-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[ListContractsResponse](#schemalistcontractsresponse)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<aside class="warning">
To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None

```php
$client = new \GuzzleHttp\Client();

// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();

try {
    $response = $client->request('GET','https://data-api.avax.network/v1/chains/{chainId}/tokens/{address}/transfers', array(
        'headers' => $headers,
        'json' => $request_body,
       )
    );
    print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
 }
 catch (\GuzzleHttp\Exception\BadResponseException $e) {
    // handle exception or api errors.
    print_r($e->getMessage());
 }

 // ...
```


`GET /v1/chains/{chainId}/tokens/{address}/transfers`

*List ERC transfers*

Lists ERC transfers for an ERC-20, ERC-721, or ERC-1155 contract address.

<h3 id="listtransfers-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|startBlock|query|number|false|The block range start number, inclusive. If endBlock is not defined when startBlock is defined, the end of the range will be the most recent block.|
|endBlock|query|number|false|The block range end number, exclusive. If startBlock is not defined when endBlock is defined, the start of the range will be the genesis block.|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 100.|
|chainId|path|string|true|A supported evm chain id or blockchain id. Use the `/chains` endpoint to get a list of supported chain ids.|
|address|path|string|true|A wallet address.|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "nextPageToken": "string",
  "transfers": [
    {
      "blockNumber": "339",
      "blockTimestamp": 1648672486,
      "blockTimestampMilliseconds": 1648672486000,
      "blockHash": "0x17533aeb5193378b9ff441d61728e7a2ebaf10f61fd5310759451627dfca2e7c",
      "txHash": "0x3e9303f81be00b4af28515dab7b914bf3dbff209ea10e7071fa24d4af0a112d4",
      "from": {
        "name": "Wrapped AVAX",
        "symbol": "WAVAX",
        "decimals": 18,
        "logoUri": "https://images.ctfassets.net/gcj8jwzm6086/5VHupNKwnDYJvqMENeV7iJ/fdd6326b7a82c8388e4ee9d4be7062d4/avalanche-avax-logo.svg",
        "address": "0x71C7656EC7ab88b098defB751B7401B5f6d8976F"
      },
      "to": {
        "name": "Wrapped AVAX",
        "symbol": "WAVAX",
        "decimals": 18,
        "logoUri": "https://images.ctfassets.net/gcj8jwzm6086/5VHupNKwnDYJvqMENeV7iJ/fdd6326b7a82c8388e4ee9d4be7062d4/avalanche-avax-logo.svg",
        "address": "0x71C7656EC7ab88b098defB751B7401B5f6d8976F"
      },
      "logIndex": 0,
      "value": "10000000000000000000",
      "erc20Token": {
        "address": "0x71C7656EC7ab88b098defB751B7401B5f6d8976F",
        "name": "Wrapped AVAX",
        "symbol": "WAVAX",
        "decimals": 18,
        "logoUri": "https://images.ctfassets.net/gcj8jwzm6086/5VHupNKwnDYJvqMENeV7iJ/fdd6326b7a82c8388e4ee9d4be7062d4/avalanche-avax-logo.svg",
        "ercType": "ERC-20",
        "price": {
          "currencyCode": "usd",
          "value": "42.42"
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

<h3 id="listtransfers-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[ListTransfersResponse](#schemalisttransfersresponse)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<aside class="warning">
To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None
</aside>

`GET /v1/chains/{chainId}/addresses/{address}/transactions`

*List transactions*

Returns a list of transactions where the given wallet address had an on-chain interaction for the given chain. The ERC-20 transfers, ERC-721 transfers, ERC-1155, and internal transactions returned are only those where the input address had an interaction. Specifically, those lists only inlcude entries where the input address was the sender (`from` field) or the receiver (`to` field) for the sub-transaction. Therefore the transactions returned from this list may not be complete representations of the on-chain data. For a complete view of a transaction use the `/chains/:chainId/transactions/:txHash` endpoint.

Filterable by block ranges.

<h3 id="listtransactions-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 100.|
|startBlock|query|number|false|The block range start number, inclusive. If endBlock is not defined when startBlock is defined, the end of the range will be the most recent block.|
|endBlock|query|number|false|The block range end number, exclusive. If startBlock is not defined when endBlock is defined, the start of the range will be the genesis block.|
|chainId|path|string|true|A supported evm chain id or blockchain id. Use the `/chains` endpoint to get a list of supported chain ids.|
|address|path|string|true|A wallet address.|
|sortOrder|query|[SortOrder](#schemasortorder)|false|The order by which to sort results. Use "asc" for ascending order, "desc" for descending order. Sorted by timestamp or the `sortBy` query parameter, if provided.|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|sortOrder|asc|
|sortOrder|desc|

> Example responses

<h3 id="listtransactions-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[ListTransactionDetailsResponse](#schemalisttransactiondetailsresponse)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None

```php
    print_r($e->getMessage());
 }

 // ...

```

```java

HttpURLConnection con = (HttpURLConnection) obj.openConnection();

    new InputStreamReader(con.getInputStream()));

while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {

}
```

```go
package main

import (
       "bytes"
       "net/http"
)

func main() {

    headers := map[string][]string{
        "Accept": []string{"application/json"},
        "x-glacier-api-key": []string{"API_KEY"},
    }

    data := bytes.NewBuffer([]byte{jsonReq})
    req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", "https://data-api.avax.network/v2/chains/{chainId}/addresses/{address}/transactions", data)
    req.Header = headers

    client := &http.Client{}
    resp, err := client.Do(req)
    // ...
}

```

`GET /v2/chains/{chainId}/addresses/{address}/transactions`

*List transactions v2*

Returns a list of transactions where the given wallet address had an on-chain interaction for the given chain. The ERC-20 transfers (with token reputation), ERC-721  transfers, ERC-1155, and internal transactions returned are only those  where the input address had an interaction. Specifically, those lists  only inlcude entries where the input address was the sender  (`from` field) or the receiver (`to` field) for the  sub-transaction. Therefore the transactions returned from this list  may not be complete representations of the on-chain data. For a  complete view of a transaction use the  `/chains/:chainId/transactions/:txHash` endpoint.

Filterable by block ranges.

<h3 id="listtransactionsv2-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 100.|
|startBlock|query|number|false|The block range start number, inclusive. If endBlock is not defined when startBlock is defined, the end of the range will be the most recent block.|
|endBlock|query|number|false|The block range end number, exclusive. If startBlock is not defined when endBlock is defined, the start of the range will be the genesis block.|
|filterSpamTokens|query|boolean|false|none|
|chainId|path|string|true|A supported evm chain id or blockchain id. Use the `/chains` endpoint to get a list of supported chain ids.|
|address|path|string|true|A wallet address.|
|sortOrder|query|[SortOrder](#schemasortorder)|false|The order by which to sort results. Use "asc" for ascending order, "desc" for descending order. Sorted by timestamp or the `sortBy` query parameter, if provided.|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|sortOrder|asc|
|sortOrder|desc|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

<h3 id="listtransactionsv2-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[ListTransactionDetailsResponseV2](#schemalisttransactiondetailsresponsev2)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<aside class="warning">
To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None
</aside>

## listNativeTransactions

<a id="opIdlistNativeTransactions"></a>

> Code samples

```shell
# You can also use wget
curl -X GET https://data-api.avax.network/v1/chains/{chainId}/addresses/{address}/transactions:listNative \
  -H 'Accept: application/json' \
  -H 'x-glacier-api-key: API_KEY'

```

```http
GET https://data-api.avax.network/v1/chains/{chainId}/addresses/{address}/transactions:listNative HTTP/1.1
Host: data-api.avax.network
Accept: application/json

```

```javascript

const headers = {
  'Accept':'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key':'API_KEY'
};

fetch('https://data-api.avax.network/v1/chains/{chainId}/addresses/{address}/transactions:listNative',
{
  method: 'GET',

  headers: headers
})
.then(function(res) {
    return res.json();
}).then(function(body) {
    console.log(body);
});

```

```ruby

}

result = RestClient.get 'https://data-api.avax.network/v1/chains/{chainId}/addresses/{address}/transactions:listNative',
  params: {
  }, headers: headers

p JSON.parse(result)

```

```php
<?php

require 'vendor/autoload.php';

$headers = array(
    'Accept' => 'application/json',
    'x-glacier-api-key' => 'API_KEY',
);

$client = new \GuzzleHttp\Client();

// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();

try {
    $response = $client->request('GET','https://data-api.avax.network/v1/chains/{chainId}/addresses/{address}/transactions:listNative', array(
        'headers' => $headers,
        'json' => $request_body,
       )
    );
    print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
 }
 catch (\GuzzleHttp\Exception\BadResponseException $e) {
    // handle exception or api errors.
    print_r($e->getMessage());
 }

 // ...
```

`GET /v1/chains/{chainId}/addresses/{address}/transactions:listNative`

*List native transactions*

Lists native transactions for an address. Filterable by block range.

<h3 id="listnativetransactions-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|startBlock|query|number|false|The block range start number, inclusive. If endBlock is not defined when startBlock is defined, the end of the range will be the most recent block.|
|endBlock|query|number|false|The block range end number, exclusive. If startBlock is not defined when endBlock is defined, the start of the range will be the genesis block.|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 100.|
|chainId|path|string|true|A supported evm chain id or blockchain id. Use the `/chains` endpoint to get a list of supported chain ids.|
|address|path|string|true|A wallet address.|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json

        "decimals": 18,

        "address": "0x71C7656EC7ab88b098defB751B7401B5f6d8976F"
```

```java
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
    response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());
```


`GET /v1/chains/{chainId}/addresses/{address}/transactions:listErc20`

*List ERC-20 transfers*

Lists ERC-20 transfers for an address. Filterable by block range.

<h3 id="listerc20transactions-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|startBlock|query|number|false|The block range start number, inclusive. If endBlock is not defined when startBlock is defined, the end of the range will be the most recent block.|
|endBlock|query|number|false|The block range end number, exclusive. If startBlock is not defined when endBlock is defined, the start of the range will be the genesis block.|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 100.|
|chainId|path|string|true|A supported evm chain id or blockchain id. Use the `/chains` endpoint to get a list of supported chain ids.|
|address|path|string|true|A wallet address.|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "nextPageToken": "string",
  "transactions": [
    {
      "blockNumber": "339",
      "blockTimestamp": 1648672486,
      "blockTimestampMilliseconds": 1648672486000,
      "blockHash": "0x17533aeb5193378b9ff441d61728e7a2ebaf10f61fd5310759451627dfca2e7c",
      "txHash": "0x3e9303f81be00b4af28515dab7b914bf3dbff209ea10e7071fa24d4af0a112d4",
      "from": {
        "name": "Wrapped AVAX",
        "symbol": "WAVAX",
        "decimals": 18,
        "logoUri": "https://images.ctfassets.net/gcj8jwzm6086/5VHupNKwnDYJvqMENeV7iJ/fdd6326b7a82c8388e4ee9d4be7062d4/avalanche-avax-logo.svg",
        "address": "0x71C7656EC7ab88b098defB751B7401B5f6d8976F"
      },
      "to": {
        "name": "Wrapped AVAX",
        "symbol": "WAVAX",
        "decimals": 18,
        "logoUri": "https://images.ctfassets.net/gcj8jwzm6086/5VHupNKwnDYJvqMENeV7iJ/fdd6326b7a82c8388e4ee9d4be7062d4/avalanche-avax-logo.svg",
        "address": "0x71C7656EC7ab88b098defB751B7401B5f6d8976F"
      },
      "logIndex": 0,
      "value": "10000000000000000000",
      "erc20Token": {
        "address": "0x71C7656EC7ab88b098defB751B7401B5f6d8976F",
        "name": "Wrapped AVAX",
        "symbol": "WAVAX",
        "decimals": 18,
        "logoUri": "https://images.ctfassets.net/gcj8jwzm6086/5VHupNKwnDYJvqMENeV7iJ/fdd6326b7a82c8388e4ee9d4be7062d4/avalanche-avax-logo.svg",
        "ercType": "ERC-20",
        "price": {
          "currencyCode": "usd",
          "value": "42.42"
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

<h3 id="listerc20transactions-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[ListErc20TransactionsResponse](#schemalisterc20transactionsresponse)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<aside class="warning">
To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None
</aside>

```ruby
}
```

```go
package main

import (
       "bytes"
       "net/http"
)

func main() {

    headers := map[string][]string{
        "Accept": []string{"application/json"},
        "x-glacier-api-key": []string{"API_KEY"},
    }

    data := bytes.NewBuffer([]byte{jsonReq})
    req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", "https://data-api.avax.network/v1/chains/{chainId}/addresses/{address}/transactions:listInternals", data)
    req.Header = headers

    client := &http.Client{}
    resp, err := client.Do(req)
    // ...
}

```

`GET /v1/chains/{chainId}/addresses/{address}/transactions:listInternals`

*List internal transactions*

Returns a list of internal transactions for an address and chain. Filterable by block range.

Note that the internal transactions list only contains `CALL` or `CALLCODE` transactions with a non-zero value and `CREATE`/`CREATE2`/`CREATE3` transactions. To get a complete list of internal transactions use the `debug_` prefixed RPC methods on an archive node.

<h3 id="listinternaltransactions-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|startBlock|query|number|false|The block range start number, inclusive. If endBlock is not defined when startBlock is defined, the end of the range will be the most recent block.|
|endBlock|query|number|false|The block range end number, exclusive. If startBlock is not defined when endBlock is defined, the start of the range will be the genesis block.|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 100.|
|chainId|path|string|true|A supported evm chain id or blockchain id. Use the `/chains` endpoint to get a list of supported chain ids.|
|address|path|string|true|A wallet address.|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "nextPageToken": "string",
  "transactions": [
    {
      "blockNumber": "339",
      "blockTimestamp": 1648672486,
      "blockTimestampMilliseconds": 1648672486000,
      "blockHash": "0x17533aeb5193378b9ff441d61728e7a2ebaf10f61fd5310759451627dfca2e7c",
      "txHash": "0x3e9303f81be00b4af28515dab7b914bf3dbff209ea10e7071fa24d4af0a112d4",
      "from": {
        "name": "Wrapped AVAX",
        "symbol": "WAVAX",
        "decimals": 18,
        "logoUri": "https://images.ctfassets.net/gcj8jwzm6086/5VHupNKwnDYJvqMENeV7iJ/fdd6326b7a82c8388e4ee9d4be7062d4/avalanche-avax-logo.svg",
        "address": "0x71C7656EC7ab88b098defB751B7401B5f6d8976F"
      },
      "to": {
        "name": "Wrapped AVAX",
        "symbol": "WAVAX",
        "decimals": 18,
        "logoUri": "https://images.ctfassets.net/gcj8jwzm6086/5VHupNKwnDYJvqMENeV7iJ/fdd6326b7a82c8388e4ee9d4be7062d4/avalanche-avax-logo.svg",
        "address": "0x71C7656EC7ab88b098defB751B7401B5f6d8976F"
      },
      "internalTxType": "UNKNOWN",
      "value": "10000000000000000000",
      "isReverted": true,
      "gasUsed": "string",
      "gasLimit": "string"
    }
  ]
}
```

<h3 id="listinternaltransactions-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[ListInternalTransactionsResponse](#schemalistinternaltransactionsresponse)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<aside class="warning">
To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None
</aside>

`GET /v1/chains/{chainId}/transactions/{txHash}`

*Get transaction*

Gets the details of a single transaction.

<h3 id="gettransaction-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|chainId|path|string|true|A supported evm chain id or blockchain id. Use the `/chains` endpoint to get a list of supported chain ids.|
|txHash|path|string|true|A transaction hash.|

<h3 id="gettransaction-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[GetTransactionResponse](#schemagettransactionresponse)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<aside class="warning">
To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None
</aside>
