```python
  'x-glacier-api-key': 'API_KEY'
}

r = requests.get('https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/vertices:listByHeight', params={
  'vertexHeight': '123'
}, headers = headers)

print(r.json())

```

```php
<?php

require 'vendor/autoload.php';

$headers = array(
    'Accept' => 'application/json',
    'x-glacier-api-key' => 'API_KEY',
);

$client = new \GuzzleHttp\Client();

// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();

try {
    $response = $client->request('GET','https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/vertices:listByHeight', array(
        'headers' => $headers,
        'json' => $request_body,
       )
    );
    print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
 }
 catch (\GuzzleHttp\Exception\BadResponseException $e) {
    // handle exception or api errors.
    print_r($e->getMessage());
 }

 // ...

```

```java
URL obj = new URL("https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/vertices:listByHeight?vertexHeight=123");
HttpURLConnection con = (HttpURLConnection) obj.openConnection();
con.setRequestMethod("GET");
int responseCode = con.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(
    new InputStreamReader(con.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
    response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

```

```go
package main

import (
       "bytes"
       "net/http"
)

func main() {

    headers := map[string][]string{
        "Accept": []string{"application/json"},
        "x-glacier-api-key": []string{"API_KEY"},
    }

    data := bytes.NewBuffer([]byte{jsonReq})
    req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", "https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/vertices:listByHeight", data)
    req.Header = headers

    client := &http.Client{}
    resp, err := client.Do(req)
    // ...
}

```

`GET /v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/vertices:listByHeight`

*List vertices by height*

Lists vertices at the given vertex height on the X-Chain.

<h3 id="getvertexbyheight-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|vertexHeight|query|integer|true|The height of a vertex.|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 100.|
|blockchainId|path|[XChainId](#schemaxchainid)|true|A primary network blockchain id or alias.|
|network|path|[Network](#schemanetwork)|true|Either mainnet or testnet/fuji.|
|sortOrder|query|[SortOrder](#schemasortorder)|false|The order by which to sort results. Use "asc" for ascending order, "desc" for descending order. Sorted by timestamp or the `sortBy` query parameter, if provided.|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|blockchainId|2oYMBNV4eNHyqk2fjjV5nVQLDbtmNJzq5s3qs3Lo6ftnC6FByM|
|blockchainId|2JVSBoinj9C2J33VntvzYtVJNZdN2NKiwwKjcumHUWEb5DbBrm|
|blockchainId|x-chain|
|network|mainnet|
|network|fuji|
|network|testnet|
|sortOrder|asc|
|sortOrder|desc|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "nextPageToken": "string",
  "vertices": [
    {
      "vertexHash": "string",
      "parentHashes": [
        "string"
      ],
      "vertexHeight": 0,
      "vertexIndex": 0,
      "vertexTimestamp": 0,
      "txCount": 0,
      "transactions": [
        "string"
      ],
      "vertexSizeBytes": 0
    }
  ],
  "chainInfo": {
    "chainName": "p-chain",
    "network": "mainnet"
  }
}
```

<h3 id="getvertexbyheight-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[ListXChainVerticesResponse](#schemalistxchainverticesresponse)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<aside class="warning">
To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None
</aside>

<h1 id="data-api-primary-network-transactions">Primary Network Transactions</h1>

Find information about transactions on Avalanche's Primary Network Chains - the P-Chain, the X-Chain, and the C-Chain.

## getTxByHash

<a id="opIdgetTxByHash"></a>

> Code samples

```shell
# You can also use wget
curl -X GET https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/transactions/{txHash} \
  -H 'Accept: application/json' \
  -H 'x-glacier-api-key: API_KEY'

```

```http
GET https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/transactions/{txHash} HTTP/1.1
Host: data-api.avax.network
Accept: application/json

```

```javascript

const headers = {
  'Accept':'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key':'API_KEY'
};

fetch('https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/transactions/{txHash}',
{
  method: 'GET',

  headers: headers
})
.then(function(res) {
    return res.json();
}).then(function(body) {
    console.log(body);
});

```

```ruby
  }, headers: headers

p JSON.parse(result)
```

`GET /v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/transactions/{txHash}`

*Get transaction*

Gets the details of a single transaction on one of the Primary Network chains.

<h3 id="gettxbyhash-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|blockchainId|path|[BlockchainId](#schemablockchainid)|true|A primary network blockchain id or alias.|
|network|path|[Network](#schemanetwork)|true|Either mainnet or testnet/fuji.|
|txHash|path|string|true|A primary network (P or X chain) transaction hash.|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|blockchainId|11111111111111111111111111111111LpoYY|
|blockchainId|2oYMBNV4eNHyqk2fjjV5nVQLDbtmNJzq5s3qs3Lo6ftnC6FByM|
|blockchainId|2JVSBoinj9C2J33VntvzYtVJNZdN2NKiwwKjcumHUWEb5DbBrm|
|blockchainId|2q9e4r6Mu3U68nU1fYjgbR6JvwrRx36CohpAX5UQxse55x1Q5|
|blockchainId|yH8D7ThNJkxmtkuv2jgBa4P1Rn3Qpr4pPr7QYNfcdoS6k6HWp|
|blockchainId|p-chain|
|blockchainId|x-chain|
|blockchainId|c-chain|
|network|mainnet|
|network|fuji|
|network|testnet|

<h3 id="gettxbyhash-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|Inline|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<h3 id="gettxbyhash-responseschema">Response Schema</h3>

|Property|Value|
|---|---|
|txType|AddValidatorTx|
|txType|AddSubnetValidatorTx|

|txType|CreateSubnetTx|

|txType|BaseTx|

|txType|ConvertSubnetToL1Tx|
|txType|RegisterL1ValidatorTx|

|txType|IncreaseL1ValidatorBalanceTx|
|txType|UNKNOWN|
|type|secp256k1|
|type|nft|
|rewardType|VALIDATOR|
|rewardType|DELEGATOR|
|rewardType|VALIDATOR_FEE|
|utxoType|STAKE|
|utxoType|TRANSFER|
|chainFormat|non-linear|

## listLatestPrimaryNetworkTransactions

<a id="opIdlistLatestPrimaryNetworkTransactions"></a>

> Code samples

```shell
# You can also use wget
curl -X GET https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/transactions \
  -H 'Accept: application/json' \
  -H 'x-glacier-api-key: API_KEY'

```

```http
GET https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/transactions HTTP/1.1
Host: data-api.avax.network
Accept: application/json

```

```javascript

const headers = {
  'Accept':'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key':'API_KEY'
};

fetch('https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/transactions',
{
  method: 'GET',

  headers: headers
})
.then(function(res) {
    return res.json();
}).then(function(body) {
    console.log(body);
});

```

```ruby

}

result = RestClient.get 'https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/transactions',
  params: {
  }, headers: headers

p JSON.parse(result)

```

```python

  'Accept': 'application/json',

}

r = requests.get('https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/transactions', headers = headers)
```

```php
<?php

require 'vendor/autoload.php';

$headers = array(
    'Accept' => 'application/json',
    'x-glacier-api-key' => 'API_KEY',
);

$client = new \GuzzleHttp\Client();

// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();

try {
    $response = $client->request('GET','https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/transactions', array(
        'headers' => $headers,
        'json' => $request_body,
       )
    );
    print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
 }
 catch (\GuzzleHttp\Exception\BadResponseException $e) {
    // handle exception or api errors.
    print_r($e->getMessage());
 }

 // ...

```

```java
URL obj = new URL("https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/transactions");
HttpURLConnection con = (HttpURLConnection) obj.openConnection();
con.setRequestMethod("GET");
int responseCode = con.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(
    new InputStreamReader(con.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
    response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

```

```go
package main

import (
       "bytes"
       "net/http"
)

func main() {

    headers := map[string][]string{
        "Accept": []string{"application/json"},
        "x-glacier-api-key": []string{"API_KEY"},
    }

    data := bytes.NewBuffer([]byte{jsonReq})
    req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", "https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/transactions", data)
    req.Header = headers

    client := &http.Client{}
    resp, err := client.Do(req)
    // ...
}

```

`GET /v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/transactions`

*List latest transactions*

Lists the latest transactions on one of the Primary Network chains.

Transactions are filterable by addresses, txTypes, and timestamps. When querying for latest transactions without an address parameter, filtering by txTypes and timestamps is not supported. An address filter must be provided to utilize txTypes and timestamp filters.

For P-Chain, you can fetch all L1 validators related transactions like ConvertSubnetToL1Tx, IncreaseL1ValidatorBalanceTx etc. using the unique L1 validation ID. These transactions are further filterable by txTypes and timestamps as well.

Given that each transaction may return a large number of UTXO objects, bounded only by the maximum transaction size, the query may return less transactions than the provided page size. The result will contain less results than the page size if the number of utxos contained in the resulting transactions reach a performance threshold.

<h3 id="listlatestprimarynetworktransactions-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|addresses|query|string|false|A comma separated list of X-Chain or P-Chain wallet addresses, starting with "avax"/"fuji", "P-avax"/"P-fuji" or "X-avax"/"X-fuji". Also accepts EVM formatted addresses starting with "0x" for C-Chain-related atomic transaction lookups.|
|l1ValidationId|query|string|false|none|
|txTypes|query|array[string]|false|Query param for filtering items based on transaction types.|
|startTimestamp|query|integer|false|Query param for retrieving items after a specific timestamp.|
|endTimestamp|query|integer|false|Query param for retrieving items before a specific timestamp.|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 100.|
|blockchainId|path|[BlockchainId](#schemablockchainid)|true|A primary network blockchain id or alias.|
|network|path|[Network](#schemanetwork)|true|Either mainnet or testnet/fuji.|
|sortOrder|query|[SortOrder](#schemasortorder)|false|The order by which to sort results. Use "asc" for ascending order, "desc" for descending order. Sorted by timestamp or the `sortBy` query parameter, if provided.|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|txTypes|AddValidatorTx|
|txTypes|AddSubnetValidatorTx|
|txTypes|AddDelegatorTx|
|txTypes|CreateChainTx|
|txTypes|CreateSubnetTx|
|txTypes|ImportTx|
|txTypes|ExportTx|
|txTypes|AdvanceTimeTx|
|txTypes|RewardValidatorTx|
|txTypes|RemoveSubnetValidatorTx|
|txTypes|TransformSubnetTx|

|txTypes|AddPermissionlessDelegatorTx|

<h3 id="listlatestprimarynetworktransactions-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|Inline|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

```php
 catch (\GuzzleHttp\Exception\BadResponseException $e) {

    print_r($e->getMessage());
 }

 // ...
```


`GET /v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/transactions:listStaking`

*List staking transactions*

Lists active staking transactions on the P-Chain for the supplied addresses.

<h3 id="listactiveprimarynetworkstakingtransactions-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|addresses|query|string|false|A comma separated list of X-Chain or P-Chain wallet addresses, starting with "avax"/"fuji", "P-avax"/"P-fuji" or "X-avax"/"X-fuji".|
|txTypes|query|array[string]|false|Query param for filtering items based on transaction types.|
|startTimestamp|query|integer|false|Query param for retrieving items after a specific timestamp.|
|endTimestamp|query|integer|false|Query param for retrieving items before a specific timestamp.|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 100.|
|blockchainId|path|[PChainId](#schemapchainid)|true|A primary network blockchain id or alias.|
|network|path|[Network](#schemanetwork)|true|Either mainnet or testnet/fuji.|
|sortOrder|query|[SortOrder](#schemasortorder)|false|The order by which to sort results. Use "asc" for ascending order, "desc" for descending order. Sorted by timestamp or the `sortBy` query parameter, if provided.|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|txTypes|AddValidatorTx|
|txTypes|AddSubnetValidatorTx|
|txTypes|AddDelegatorTx|
|txTypes|CreateChainTx|
|txTypes|CreateSubnetTx|
|txTypes|ImportTx|
|txTypes|ExportTx|
|txTypes|AdvanceTimeTx|
|txTypes|RewardValidatorTx|
|txTypes|RemoveSubnetValidatorTx|
|txTypes|TransformSubnetTx|
|txTypes|AddPermissionlessValidatorTx|
|txTypes|AddPermissionlessDelegatorTx|
|txTypes|BaseTx|
|txTypes|TransferSubnetOwnershipTx|
|txTypes|ConvertSubnetToL1Tx|
|txTypes|RegisterL1ValidatorTx|
|txTypes|SetL1ValidatorWeightTx|
|txTypes|DisableL1ValidatorTx|
|txTypes|IncreaseL1ValidatorBalanceTx|
|txTypes|UNKNOWN|
|txTypes|CreateAssetTx|
|txTypes|OperationTx|
|blockchainId|11111111111111111111111111111111LpoYY|
|blockchainId|p-chain|
|network|mainnet|
|network|fuji|
|network|testnet|
|sortOrder|asc|
|sortOrder|desc|

<h3 id="listactiveprimarynetworkstakingtransactions-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[ListPChainTransactionsResponse](#schemalistpchaintransactionsresponse)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<aside class="warning">
To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None
</aside>

## listAssetTransactions

<a id="opIdlistAssetTransactions"></a>

> Code samples

```shell
# You can also use wget
curl -X GET https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/assets/{assetId}/transactions \
  -H 'Accept: application/json' \
  -H 'x-glacier-api-key: API_KEY'

```

```http
GET https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/assets/{assetId}/transactions HTTP/1.1
Host: data-api.avax.network
Accept: application/json

```

```javascript

const headers = {
  'Accept':'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key':'API_KEY'
};

fetch('https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/assets/{assetId}/transactions',
{
  method: 'GET',

  headers: headers
})
.then(function(res) {
    return res.json();
}).then(function(body) {
    console.log(body);
});

```

```ruby
}

p JSON.parse(result)
```

`GET /v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/assets/{assetId}/transactions`

*List asset transactions*

Lists asset transactions corresponding to the given asset id on the X-Chain.

<h3 id="listassettransactions-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|txTypes|query|array[string]|false|Query param for filtering items based on transaction types.|
|startTimestamp|query|integer|false|Query param for retrieving items after a specific timestamp.|
|endTimestamp|query|integer|false|Query param for retrieving items before a specific timestamp.|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 100.|
|blockchainId|path|[XChainId](#schemaxchainid)|true|A primary network blockchain id or alias.|
|network|path|[Network](#schemanetwork)|true|Either mainnet or testnet/fuji.|
|assetId|path|string|true|Asset ID for any asset on X-Chain|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|txTypes|AddValidatorTx|
|txTypes|AddSubnetValidatorTx|
|txTypes|AddDelegatorTx|
|txTypes|CreateChainTx|
|txTypes|CreateSubnetTx|

<h3 id="listassettransactions-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[ListXChainTransactionsResponse](#schemalistxchaintransactionsresponse)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<aside class="warning">
To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None
</aside>

<h1 id="data-api-primary-network-balances">Primary Network Balances</h1>

Find information about balances on Avalanche's Primary Network Chains - the P-Chain, the X-Chain, and the C-Chain.

## getBalancesByAddresses

<a id="opIdgetBalancesByAddresses"></a>

> Code samples

```shell
# You can also use wget
curl -X GET https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/balances \
  -H 'Accept: application/json' \
  -H 'x-glacier-api-key: API_KEY'

```

```http
GET https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/balances HTTP/1.1
Host: data-api.avax.network
Accept: application/json

```

```javascript

const headers = {
  'Accept':'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key':'API_KEY'
};

fetch('https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/balances',
{
  method: 'GET',

  headers: headers
})
.then(function(res) {
    return res.json();
}).then(function(body) {
    console.log(body);
});

```

```ruby
require 'rest-client'
require 'json'

headers = {
  'Accept' => 'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key' => 'API_KEY'
}

result = RestClient.get 'https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/balances',
  params: {
  }, headers: headers

p JSON.parse(result)

```

```python
import requests
headers = {
  'Accept': 'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key': 'API_KEY'
}

r = requests.get('https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/balances', headers = headers)

print(r.json())

```

```php
<?php

require 'vendor/autoload.php';

$headers = array(
    'Accept' => 'application/json',
    'x-glacier-api-key' => 'API_KEY',
);

$client = new \GuzzleHttp\Client();

// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();

try {
    $response = $client->request('GET','https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/balances', array(
        'headers' => $headers,
        'json' => $request_body,
       )
    );
    print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
 }
 catch (\GuzzleHttp\Exception\BadResponseException $e) {
    // handle exception or api errors.
    print_r($e->getMessage());
 }

 // ...

```

```java
URL obj = new URL("https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/balances");
HttpURLConnection con = (HttpURLConnection) obj.openConnection();
con.setRequestMethod("GET");
int responseCode = con.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(
    new InputStreamReader(con.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
    response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

```

```go
package main

import (
       "bytes"
       "net/http"
)

func main() {

    headers := map[string][]string{
        "Accept": []string{"application/json"},
        "x-glacier-api-key": []string{"API_KEY"},
    }

    data := bytes.NewBuffer([]byte{jsonReq})
    req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", "https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/balances", data)
    req.Header = headers

    client := &http.Client{}
    resp, err := client.Do(req)
    // ...
}

```

`GET /v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/balances`

*Get balances*

Gets primary network balances for one of the Primary Network chains for the supplied addresses.

C-Chain balances returned are only the shared atomic memory balance. For EVM balance, use the `/v1/chains/:chainId/addresses/:addressId/balances:getNative` endpoint.

<h3 id="getbalancesbyaddresses-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|blockTimestamp|query|integer|false|An epoch timestamp in seconds. Balance will be calculated at this timestamp.|
|addresses|query|string|false|A comma separated list of X-Chain or P-Chain wallet addresses, starting with "avax"/"fuji", "P-avax"/"P-fuji" or "X-avax"/"X-fuji".|
|blockchainId|path|[BlockchainId](#schemablockchainid)|true|A primary network blockchain id or alias.|
|network|path|[Network](#schemanetwork)|true|Either mainnet or testnet/fuji.|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|blockchainId|11111111111111111111111111111111LpoYY|
|blockchainId|2oYMBNV4eNHyqk2fjjV5nVQLDbtmNJzq5s3qs3Lo6ftnC6FByM|
|blockchainId|2JVSBoinj9C2J33VntvzYtVJNZdN2NKiwwKjcumHUWEb5DbBrm|
|blockchainId|2q9e4r6Mu3U68nU1fYjgbR6JvwrRx36CohpAX5UQxse55x1Q5|
|blockchainId|yH8D7ThNJkxmtkuv2jgBa4P1Rn3Qpr4pPr7QYNfcdoS6k6HWp|
|blockchainId|p-chain|
|blockchainId|x-chain|
|blockchainId|c-chain|
|network|mainnet|
|network|fuji|
|network|testnet|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

<h3 id="getbalancesbyaddresses-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|Inline|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

|Property|Value|
|---|---|

|type|nft|

|chainName|c-chain|

<h1 id="data-api-primary-network-utxos">Primary Network UTXOs</h1>

`GET /v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/utxos`

*List UTXOs*

Lists UTXOs on one of the Primary Network chains for the supplied addresses.

<h3 id="getutxosbyaddresses-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|addresses|query|string|false|A comma separated list of X-Chain or P-Chain wallet addresses, starting with "avax"/"fuji", "P-avax"/"P-fuji" or "X-avax"/"X-fuji".|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 100.|
|blockchainId|path|[BlockchainId](#schemablockchainid)|true|A primary network blockchain id or alias.|
|network|path|[Network](#schemanetwork)|true|Either mainnet or testnet/fuji.|
|assetId|query|string|false|Asset ID for any asset (only applicable X-Chain)|
|minUtxoAmount|query|number|false|The minimum UTXO amount in nAVAX (inclusive), used to filter the set of UTXOs being returned. Default is 0.|
|includeSpent|query|boolean|false|Boolean filter to include spent UTXOs.|
|sortBy|query|[UtxosSortByOption](#schemautxossortbyoption)|false|Which property to sort by, in conjunction with sortOrder.|
|sortOrder|query|[SortOrder](#schemasortorder)|false|The order by which to sort results. Use "asc" for ascending order, "desc" for descending order. Sorted by timestamp or the `sortBy` query parameter, if provided.|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|blockchainId|11111111111111111111111111111111LpoYY|
|blockchainId|2oYMBNV4eNHyqk2fjjV5nVQLDbtmNJzq5s3qs3Lo6ftnC6FByM|
|blockchainId|2JVSBoinj9C2J33VntvzYtVJNZdN2NKiwwKjcumHUWEb5DbBrm|
|blockchainId|2q9e4r6Mu3U68nU1fYjgbR6JvwrRx36CohpAX5UQxse55x1Q5|
|blockchainId|yH8D7ThNJkxmtkuv2jgBa4P1Rn3Qpr4pPr7QYNfcdoS6k6HWp|
|blockchainId|p-chain|
|blockchainId|x-chain|
|blockchainId|c-chain|
|network|mainnet|
|network|fuji|
|network|testnet|
|sortBy|timestamp|
|sortBy|amount|
|sortOrder|asc|
|sortOrder|desc|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "nextPageToken": "string",
  "utxos": [
    {
      "addresses": [
        "avax1qm2a25eytsrj235hxg6jc0mwk99tss64eqevsw"
      ],
      "asset": {
        "assetId": "th5aLdWLi32yS9ED6uLGoMMubqHjzMsXhKWwzP6yZTYQKYzof",
        "name": "Avalanche",
        "symbol": "AVAX",
        "denomination": 9,
        "type": "secp256k1",
        "amount": "5001000"
      },
      "consumedOnChainId": "2oYMBNV4eNHyqk2fjjV5nVQLDbtmNJzq5s3qs3Lo6ftnC6FByM",
      "consumingTxHash": "3j9JpF1aGuQtLLbo3YkvvKkWrCZViXZjdeJQWUSEY5hcqUn2c",
      "createdOnChainId": "2oYMBNV4eNHyqk2fjjV5nVQLDbtmNJzq5s3qs3Lo6ftnC6FByM",
      "utxoId": "68vLva9kfKUsX88ZPM8jdbf8qHUZRoZSFH6NdpAVTspkfrXDC",
      "utxoBytes": "string",
      "amount": "string",
      "assetId": "string",
      "blockNumber": "string",

  }
}
```

`POST /v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/utxos`

*List UTXOs v2 - Supports querying for more addresses*

Lists UTXOs on one of the Primary Network chains for the supplied addresses. This v2 route supports increased page size and address limit.

> Body parameter

```json
{
  "addresses": "P-avax1abc123,X-avax1def456,C-avax1ghi789"
}
```

<h3 id="getutxosbyaddressesv2-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 1024.|
|blockchainId|path|[BlockchainId](#schemablockchainid)|true|A primary network blockchain id or alias.|
|network|path|[Network](#schemanetwork)|true|Either mainnet or testnet/fuji.|
|assetId|query|string|false|Asset ID for any asset (only applicable X-Chain)|
|minUtxoAmount|query|number|false|The minimum UTXO amount in nAVAX (inclusive), used to filter the set of UTXOs being returned. Default is 0.|
|includeSpent|query|boolean|false|Boolean filter to include spent UTXOs.|
|sortBy|query|[UtxosSortByOption](#schemautxossortbyoption)|false|Which property to sort by, in conjunction with sortOrder.|
|sortOrder|query|[SortOrder](#schemasortorder)|false|The order by which to sort results. Use "asc" for ascending order, "desc" for descending order. Sorted by timestamp or the `sortBy` query parameter, if provided.|
|body|body|[PrimaryNetworkAddressesBodyDto](#schemaprimarynetworkaddressesbodydto)|true|none|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|blockchainId|11111111111111111111111111111111LpoYY|
|blockchainId|2oYMBNV4eNHyqk2fjjV5nVQLDbtmNJzq5s3qs3Lo6ftnC6FByM|
|blockchainId|2JVSBoinj9C2J33VntvzYtVJNZdN2NKiwwKjcumHUWEb5DbBrm|
|blockchainId|2q9e4r6Mu3U68nU1fYjgbR6JvwrRx36CohpAX5UQxse55x1Q5|
|blockchainId|yH8D7ThNJkxmtkuv2jgBa4P1Rn3Qpr4pPr7QYNfcdoS6k6HWp|
|blockchainId|p-chain|
|blockchainId|x-chain|
|blockchainId|c-chain|
|network|mainnet|
|network|fuji|
|network|testnet|
|sortBy|timestamp|
|sortBy|amount|
|sortOrder|asc|
|sortOrder|desc|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "nextPageToken": "string",
  "utxos": [
    {
      "addresses": [
        "avax1qm2a25eytsrj235hxg6jc0mwk99tss64eqevsw"
      ],
      "asset": {
        "assetId": "th5aLdWLi32yS9ED6uLGoMMubqHjzMsXhKWwzP6yZTYQKYzof",

        "denomination": 9,
        "type": "secp256k1",
        "amount": "5001000"
      },
      "consumedOnChainId": "2oYMBNV4eNHyqk2fjjV5nVQLDbtmNJzq5s3qs3Lo6ftnC6FByM",
      "consumingTxHash": "3j9JpF1aGuQtLLbo3YkvvKkWrCZViXZjdeJQWUSEY5hcqUn2c",
      "createdOnChainId": "2oYMBNV4eNHyqk2fjjV5nVQLDbtmNJzq5s3qs3Lo6ftnC6FByM",
      "utxoId": "68vLva9kfKUsX88ZPM8jdbf8qHUZRoZSFH6NdpAVTspkfrXDC",
      "utxoBytes": "string",
      "amount": "string",
      "assetId": "string",
      "blockNumber": "string",
      "blockTimestamp": 0,
      "consumingBlockNumber": "string",
      "consumingBlockTimestamp": 0,
      "platformLocktime": 0,
      "outputIndex": 0,
      "rewardType": "VALIDATOR",
      "stakeableLocktime": 0,
      "staked": true,
      "threshold": 0,
      "txHash": "string",
      "utxoEndTimestamp": 0,
      "utxoStartTimestamp": 0,
      "utxoType": "STAKE"
    }
  ],
  "chainInfo": {
    "chainName": "p-chain",
    "network": "mainnet"
  }
}
```

`GET /v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/lastActivityTimestampByAddresses`

*Get last activity timestamp by addresses*

Gets the last activity timestamp for the supplied addresses on one of the Primary Network chains.

<h3 id="getlastactivitytimestampbyaddresses-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|addresses|query|string|false|A comma separated list of X-Chain or P-Chain wallet addresses, starting with "avax"/"fuji", "P-avax"/"P-fuji" or "X-avax"/"X-fuji".|
|blockchainId|path|[BlockchainId](#schemablockchainid)|true|A primary network blockchain id or alias.|
|network|path|[Network](#schemanetwork)|true|Either mainnet or testnet/fuji.|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|blockchainId|11111111111111111111111111111111LpoYY|
|blockchainId|2oYMBNV4eNHyqk2fjjV5nVQLDbtmNJzq5s3qs3Lo6ftnC6FByM|
|blockchainId|2JVSBoinj9C2J33VntvzYtVJNZdN2NKiwwKjcumHUWEb5DbBrm|
|blockchainId|2q9e4r6Mu3U68nU1fYjgbR6JvwrRx36CohpAX5UQxse55x1Q5|
|blockchainId|yH8D7ThNJkxmtkuv2jgBa4P1Rn3Qpr4pPr7QYNfcdoS6k6HWp|
|blockchainId|p-chain|
|blockchainId|x-chain|
|blockchainId|c-chain|
|network|mainnet|
|network|fuji|
|network|testnet|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "timestamp": 0,
  "blockNumber": "string",
  "txHash": "string",
  "utxoId": "string",
  "isConsumed": true,
  "chainName": "p-chain",
  "network": "mainnet"
}
```

<h3 id="getlastactivitytimestampbyaddresses-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|Inline|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<h3 id="getlastactivitytimestampbyaddresses-responseschema">Response Schema</h3>

Status Code **200**

|Name|Type|Required|Restrictions|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|» timestamp|number|true|none|Unix timestamp in seconds at which the last activity occurred.|
|» blockNumber|string|true|none|Block height at which the last activity occurred.|
|» txHash|string|true|none|Transaction hash of the transaction that created or consumed the address' UTXOs.|
|» utxoId|string|true|none|UTXO ID of the UTXO that was created or consumed.|
|» isConsumed|boolean|true|none|Whether the last activity was a consumption of an existing UTXO.|
|» chainName|[PrimaryNetworkChainName](#schemaprimarynetworkchainname)|true|none|none|
|» network|[PrimaryNetworkType](#schemaprimarynetworktype)|true|none|none|

#### Enumerated Values

|Property|Value|
|---|---|
|chainName|p-chain|
|chainName|x-chain|
|chainName|c-chain|
|network|mainnet|
|network|fuji|

To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None
</aside>


`POST /v1/networks/{network}/blockchains/{blockchainId}/lastActivityTimestampByAddresses`

*Get last activity timestamp by addresses v2*

Gets the last activity timestamp for the supplied addresses on one of the Primary Network chains. V2 route supports querying for more addresses.

> Body parameter

```json
{
  "addresses": "P-avax1abc123,X-avax1def456,C-avax1ghi789"
}
```

<h3 id="getlastactivitytimestampbyaddressesv2-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|blockchainId|path|[BlockchainId](#schemablockchainid)|true|A primary network blockchain id or alias.|
|network|path|[Network](#schemanetwork)|true|Either mainnet or testnet/fuji.|
|body|body|[PrimaryNetworkAddressesBodyDto](#schemaprimarynetworkaddressesbodydto)|true|none|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|blockchainId|11111111111111111111111111111111LpoYY|
|blockchainId|2oYMBNV4eNHyqk2fjjV5nVQLDbtmNJzq5s3qs3Lo6ftnC6FByM|
|blockchainId|2JVSBoinj9C2J33VntvzYtVJNZdN2NKiwwKjcumHUWEb5DbBrm|
|blockchainId|2q9e4r6Mu3U68nU1fYjgbR6JvwrRx36CohpAX5UQxse55x1Q5|
|blockchainId|yH8D7ThNJkxmtkuv2jgBa4P1Rn3Qpr4pPr7QYNfcdoS6k6HWp|
|blockchainId|p-chain|
|blockchainId|x-chain|
|blockchainId|c-chain|
|network|mainnet|
|network|fuji|
|network|testnet|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "timestamp": 0,
  "blockNumber": "string",
  "txHash": "string",
  "utxoId": "string",
  "isConsumed": true,
  "chainName": "p-chain",
  "network": "mainnet"
}
```

<h3 id="getlastactivitytimestampbyaddressesv2-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|Inline|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<h3 id="getlastactivitytimestampbyaddressesv2-responseschema">Response Schema</h3>

Status Code **200**

|Name|Type|Required|Restrictions|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|» timestamp|number|true|none|Unix timestamp in seconds at which the last activity occurred.|
|» blockNumber|string|true|none|Block height at which the last activity occurred.|
|» txHash|string|true|none|Transaction hash of the transaction that created or consumed the address' UTXOs.|
|» utxoId|string|true|none|UTXO ID of the UTXO that was created or consumed.|
|» isConsumed|boolean|true|none|Whether the last activity was a consumption of an existing UTXO.|
|» chainName|[PrimaryNetworkChainName](#schemaprimarynetworkchainname)|true|none|none|
|» network|[PrimaryNetworkType](#schemaprimarynetworktype)|true|none|none|

#### Enumerated Values

|Property|Value|
|---|---|
|chainName|p-chain|
|chainName|x-chain|
|chainName|c-chain|
|network|mainnet|
|network|fuji|

<aside class="warning">
To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None
</aside>

<h1 id="data-api-primary-network-rewards">Primary Network Rewards</h1>

Find information about rewards on Avalanche's Primary Network.

## listPendingPrimaryNetworkRewards

<a id="opIdlistPendingPrimaryNetworkRewards"></a>

> Code samples

```shell
# You can also use wget
curl -X GET https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/rewards:listPending \
  -H 'Accept: application/json' \
  -H 'x-glacier-api-key: API_KEY'

```

```http
GET https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/rewards:listPending HTTP/1.1
Host: data-api.avax.network
Accept: application/json

```

```javascript

const headers = {
  'Accept':'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key':'API_KEY'
};

fetch('https://data-api.avax.network/v1/networks/{network}/rewards:listPending',
{
  method: 'GET',

  headers: headers
})
.then(function(res) {
    return res.json();
}).then(function(body) {
    console.log(body);
});

```

`GET /v1/networks/{network}/rewards:listPending`

*List pending rewards*

Lists pending rewards on the Primary Network for the supplied addresses.

<h3 id="listpendingprimarynetworkrewards-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|addresses|query|string|false|A comma separated list of X-Chain or P-Chain wallet addresses, starting with "avax"/"fuji", "P-avax"/"P-fuji" or "X-avax"/"X-fuji". One of addresses or nodeIds is required.|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 100.|
|network|path|[Network](#schemanetwork)|true|Either mainnet or testnet/fuji.|
|nodeIds|query|string|false|A comma separated list of node ids to filter by. One of addresses or nodeIds is required.|
|sortOrder|query|[SortOrder](#schemasortorder)|false|The order by which to sort results. Use "asc" for ascending order, "desc" for descending order. Sorted by timestamp or the `sortBy` query parameter, if provided.|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|network|mainnet|
|network|fuji|
|network|testnet|
|sortOrder|asc|
|sortOrder|desc|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "nextPageToken": "string",
  "pendingRewards": [
    {
      "addresses": [
        "avax1h2ccj9f5ay5acl6tyn9mwmw32p8wref8vl8ctg"
      ],
      "txHash": "string",
      "amountStaked": "string",
      "nodeId": "string",
      "startTimestamp": 0,
      "endTimestamp": 0,
      "rewardType": "VALIDATOR",
      "progress": 0,
      "estimatedReward": {
        "assetId": "th5aLdWLi32yS9ED6uLGoMMubqHjzMsXhKWwzP6yZTYQKYzof",
        "name": "Avalanche",
        "symbol": "AVAX",
        "denomination": 9,
        "type": "secp256k1",
        "amount": "5001000"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

<h3 id="listpendingprimarynetworkrewards-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[ListPendingRewardsResponse](#schemalistpendingrewardsresponse)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:

`GET /v1/networks/{network}/rewards`

*List historical rewards*

Lists historical rewards on the Primary Network for the supplied addresses.

<h3 id="listhistoricalprimarynetworkrewards-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|addresses|query|string|false|A comma separated list of X-Chain or P-Chain wallet addresses, starting with "avax"/"fuji", "P-avax"/"P-fuji" or "X-avax"/"X-fuji". Optional, but at least one of addresses or nodeIds is required.|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 100.|
|network|path|[Network](#schemanetwork)|true|Either mainnet or testnet/fuji.|
|nodeIds|query|string|false|A comma separated list of node ids to filter by. Optional, but at least one of addresses or nodeIds is required.|
|sortOrder|query|[SortOrder](#schemasortorder)|false|The order by which to sort results. Use "asc" for ascending order, "desc" for descending order. Sorted by timestamp or the `sortBy` query parameter, if provided.|
|currency|query|[CurrencyCode](#schemacurrencycode)|false|The currency that return values should use. Defaults to USD.|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|network|mainnet|
|network|fuji|
|network|testnet|
|sortOrder|asc|
|sortOrder|desc|
|currency|usd|
|currency|eur|
|currency|aud|
|currency|cad|
|currency|chf|
|currency|clp|
|currency|cny|
|currency|czk|
|currency|dkk|
|currency|gbp|
|currency|hkd|
|currency|huf|
|currency|jpy|
|currency|nzd|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "nextPageToken": "string",
  "historicalRewards": [
    {
      "addresses": [
        "avax1h2ccj9f5ay5acl6tyn9mwmw32p8wref8vl8ctg"
      ],
      "txHash": "string",
      "amountStaked": "string",
      "nodeId": "string",
      "startTimestamp": 0,
      "endTimestamp": 0,
      "rewardType": "VALIDATOR",
      "utxoId": "string",
      "outputIndex": 0,
      "reward": {
        "assetId": "th5aLdWLi32yS9ED6uLGoMMubqHjzMsXhKWwzP6yZTYQKYzof",
        "name": "Avalanche",
        "symbol": "AVAX",
        "denomination": 9,
        "type": "secp256k1",
        "amount": "5001000",
        "historicalPrice": {
          "currencyCode": "usd",
          "value": "42.42"
        }
      },
      "rewardTxHash": "string"
    }
  ]
}
```

<h3 id="listhistoricalprimarynetworkrewards-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[ListHistoricalRewardsResponse](#schemalisthistoricalrewardsresponse)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

```ruby
  'x-glacier-api-key' => 'API_KEY'

  params: {
  }, headers: headers
```

`POST /v1/chains/{chainId}/nfts/collections/{address}/tokens/{tokenId}:reindex`

*Reindex NFT metadata*

Triggers reindexing of token metadata for an NFT token. Reindexing can only be called once per hour for each NFT token.

<h3 id="reindexnft-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|chainId|path|string|true|A supported evm chain id or blockchain id. Use the `/chains` endpoint to get a list of supported chain ids.|
|address|path|string|true|Contract address on the relevant chain.|
|tokenId|path|string|true|TokenId on the contract|

> Example responses

> 400 Response

```json
{
  "message": "string",
  "statusCode": 400,
  "error": "Bad Request"
}
```

<h3 id="reindexnft-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|201|[Created](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.2)|Successful response|None|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<aside class="warning">
To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None
</aside>

`GET /v1/chains/{chainId}/nfts/collections/{address}/tokens`

*List tokens*

Lists tokens for an NFT contract.

<h3 id="listtokens-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 100.|
|chainId|path|string|true|A supported evm chain id or blockchain id. Use the `/chains` endpoint to get a list of supported chain ids.|
|address|path|string|true|Contract address on the relevant chain.|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "nextPageToken": "string",
  "tokens": [
    {
      "address": "0x71C7656EC7ab88b098defB751B7401B5f6d8976F",
      "name": "Wrapped AVAX",
      "symbol": "WAVAX",
      "ercType": "ERC-721",
      "tokenId": "string",
      "tokenUri": "string",
      "metadata": {
        "indexStatus": "UNKNOWN",
        "metadataLastUpdatedTimestamp": 0,
        "name": "string",
        "symbol": "string",
        "imageUri": "string",
        "description": "string",
        "animationUri": "string",
        "externalUrl": "string",
        "background": "string",
        "attributes": "string"
      },
      "ownerAddress": "0x71C7656EC7ab88b098defB751B7401B5f6d8976F"
    }
  ]
}
```

<h3 id="listtokens-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[ListNftTokens](#schemalistnfttokens)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:

`GET /v1/chains/{chainId}/nfts/collections/{address}/tokens/{tokenId}`

*Get token details*

Gets token details for a specific token of an NFT contract.

<h3 id="gettokendetails-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|chainId|path|string|true|A supported evm chain id or blockchain id. Use the `/chains` endpoint to get a list of supported chain ids.|
|address|path|string|true|Contract address on the relevant chain.|
|tokenId|path|string|true|TokenId on the contract|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "address": "0x71C7656EC7ab88b098defB751B7401B5f6d8976F",
  "name": "Wrapped AVAX",
  "symbol": "WAVAX",
  "ercType": "ERC-721",
  "tokenId": "string",
  "tokenUri": "string",
  "metadata": {
    "indexStatus": "UNKNOWN",
    "metadataLastUpdatedTimestamp": 0,
    "name": "string",
    "symbol": "string",
    "imageUri": "string",
    "description": "string",
    "animationUri": "string",
    "externalUrl": "string",
    "background": "string",
    "attributes": "string"
  },
  "ownerAddress": "0x71C7656EC7ab88b098defB751B7401B5f6d8976F"
}
```

<h3 id="gettokendetails-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|Inline|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<h3 id="gettokendetails-responseschema">Response Schema</h3>

#### Enumerated Values

|Property|Value|
|---|---|
|ercType|ERC-721|
|indexStatus|UNKNOWN|
|indexStatus|MISSING_TOKEN|
|indexStatus|INVALID_TOKEN_URI|
|indexStatus|INVALID_TOKEN_URI_SCHEME|
|indexStatus|UNREACHABLE_TOKEN_URI|
|indexStatus|THROTTLED_TOKEN_URI|
|indexStatus|METADATA_CONTENT_TOO_LARGE|
|indexStatus|INVALID_METADATA|
|indexStatus|INVALID_METADATA_JSON|
|indexStatus|INDEXED|
|indexStatus|UNINDEXED|
|ercType|ERC-1155|
|indexStatus|UNKNOWN|
|indexStatus|MISSING_TOKEN|
|indexStatus|INVALID_TOKEN_URI|
|indexStatus|INVALID_TOKEN_URI_SCHEME|
|indexStatus|UNREACHABLE_TOKEN_URI|
|indexStatus|THROTTLED_TOKEN_URI|
|indexStatus|METADATA_CONTENT_TOO_LARGE|
|indexStatus|INVALID_METADATA|
|indexStatus|INVALID_METADATA_JSON|
|indexStatus|INDEXED|
|indexStatus|UNINDEXED|

`GET /v1/operations/{operationId}`

*Get operation*

Gets operation details for the given operation id.

<h3 id="getoperationresult-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|operationId|path|string|true|UUID of given operation|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "operationId": "string",
  "operationType": "TRANSACTION_EXPORT_PRIMARY_NETWORK",
  "operationStatus": "RUNNING",
  "message": "string",
  "metadata": {
    "code": "ErrInvalidRequest",
    "downloadUrl": "string",
    "nextDate": "string"
  },
  "createdAtTimestamp": 0,
  "updatedAtTimestamp": 0
}
```

<h3 id="getoperationresult-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[OperationStatusResponse](#schemaoperationstatusresponse)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<aside class="warning">
To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None
</aside>

```shell

  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \

  -H 'x-glacier-api-key: API_KEY'

```

```http
POST https://data-api.avax.network/v1/operations/transactions:export HTTP/1.1
Host: data-api.avax.network
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

```

```javascript
const inputBody = '{
  "type": "TRANSACTION_EXPORT_EVM",
  "firstDate": "2023-05-01",
  "lastDate": "2023-05-02",
  "startDate": "2023-05-01",
  "endDate": "2023-05-02",
  "options": {
    "addresses": [
      "string"
    ],
    "includeChains": [
      "43114"
    ]
  }
}';
const headers = {
  'Content-Type':'application/json',
  'Accept':'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key':'API_KEY'
};

fetch('https://data-api.avax.network/v1/operations/transactions:export',
{
  method: 'POST',
  body: inputBody,
  headers: headers
})
.then(function(res) {
    return res.json();
}).then(function(body) {
    console.log(body);
});

```

```ruby
require 'rest-client'
require 'json'

headers = {
  'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
  'Accept' => 'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key' => 'API_KEY'
}

result = RestClient.post 'https://data-api.avax.network/v1/operations/transactions:export',

  }, headers: headers

```

```python
print(r.json())
```

```php

    'Content-Type' => 'application/json',

    'x-glacier-api-key' => 'API_KEY',
);

$client = new \GuzzleHttp\Client();

// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();

try {
    $response = $client->request('POST','https://data-api.avax.network/v1/operations/transactions:export', array(

       )
    );

 catch (\GuzzleHttp\Exception\BadResponseException $e) {

}
```
```java
in.close();
```

```go
package main

       "bytes"
       "net/http"
)

func main() {

    headers := map[string][]string{
        "Content-Type": []string{"application/json"},
        "Accept": []string{"application/json"},
        "x-glacier-api-key": []string{"API_KEY"},
    }
```
