```go
    req, err := http.NewRequest("POST", "https://data-api.avax.network/v1/operations/transactions:export", data)
    req.Header = headers

    client := &http.Client{}
    resp, err := client.Do(req)
    // ...
}

```

`POST /v1/operations/transactions:export`

*Create transaction export operation*

Trigger a transaction export operation with given parameters.

The transaction export operation runs asynchronously in the background. The status of the job can be retrieved from the `/v1/operations/:operationId` endpoint using the `operationId` returned from this endpoint.

> Body parameter

```json
{
  "type": "TRANSACTION_EXPORT_EVM",
  "firstDate": "2023-05-01",
  "lastDate": "2023-05-02",
  "startDate": "2023-05-01",
  "endDate": "2023-05-02",
  "options": {
    "addresses": [
      "string"
    ],
    "includeChains": [
      "43114"
    ]
  }
}
```

<h3 id="posttransactionexportjob-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|body|body|any|true|none|

> Example responses

> 201 Response

```json
{
  "operationId": "string",
  "operationType": "TRANSACTION_EXPORT_PRIMARY_NETWORK",
  "operationStatus": "RUNNING",
  "message": "string",
  "metadata": {
    "code": "ErrInvalidRequest",
    "downloadUrl": "string",
    "nextDate": "string"
  },
  "createdAtTimestamp": 0,
  "updatedAtTimestamp": 0
}
```

<h3 id="posttransactionexportjob-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|201|[Created](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.2)|Successful response|[OperationStatusResponse](#schemaoperationstatusresponse)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response

`GET /v1/health-check`

*Get the health of the service*

Check the health of the service. This checks the read and write health of the database and cache.

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "status": "error",
  "info": {
    "database": {
      "status": "up"
    }
  },
  "error": {},
  "details": {
    "database": {
      "status": "up"
    }
  }
}
```

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|The health of the service. This checks the read and write health of the database and cache.|[HealthCheckResultDto](#schemahealthcheckresultdto)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require

    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may

```go
    client := &http.Client{}
    resp, err := client.Do(req)
    // ...
}

```

`GET /v1/teleporter/messages/{messageId}`

**[Deprecated]** Gets a teleporter message by message ID.

⚠️ **This operation will be removed in a future release.  Please use /v1/icm/messages/:messageId endpoint instead** .

<h3 id="getteleportermessage-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|messageId|path|string|true|The message ID of the teleporter message.|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "messageId": "string",
  "teleporterContractAddress": "string",
  "sourceBlockchainId": "string",
  "destinationBlockchainId": "string",
  "sourceEvmChainId": "string",
  "destinationEvmChainId": "string",
  "messageNonce": "string",
  "from": "string",
  "to": "string",
  "data": "string",
  "messageExecuted": true,
  "receipts": [
    {
      "receivedMessageNonce": "string",
      "relayerRewardAddress": "string"
    }
  ],
  "receiptDelivered": true,
  "rewardDetails": {
    "address": "0x71C7656EC7ab88b098defB751B7401B5f6d8976F",
    "name": "Wrapped AVAX",
    "symbol": "WAVAX",
    "decimals": 18,
    "logoUri": "https://images.ctfassets.net/gcj8jwzm6086/5VHupNKwnDYJvqMENeV7iJ/fdd6326b7a82c8388e4ee9d4be7062d4/avalanche-avax-logo.svg",
    "ercType": "ERC-20",
    "price": {
      "currencyCode": "usd",
      "value": "42.42"
    },
    "value": "string"
  },
  "sourceTransaction": {
    "txHash": "string",
    "timestamp": 0,
    "gasSpent": "string"
  },
  "status": "pending"
}
```

<h3 id="getteleportermessage-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|Inline|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<h3 id="getteleportermessage-responseschema">Response Schema</h3>

#### Enumerated Values

|Property|Value|
|---|---|
|ercType|ERC-20|
|currencyCode|usd|
|currencyCode|eur|
|currencyCode|aud|
|currencyCode|cad|
|currencyCode|chf|
|currencyCode|clp|
|currencyCode|cny|
|currencyCode|czk|
|currencyCode|dkk|
|currencyCode|gbp|
|currencyCode|hkd|
|currencyCode|huf|
|currencyCode|jpy|
|currencyCode|nzd|
|status|pending|
|status|delivered|
|status|delivered_source_not_indexed|

To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None
</aside>

## listTeleporterMessages

<a id="opIdlistTeleporterMessages"></a>

> Code samples

```shell
# You can also use wget
curl -X GET https://data-api.avax.network/v1/teleporter/messages \
  -H 'Accept: application/json' \
  -H 'x-glacier-api-key: API_KEY'

```

```http
GET https://data-api.avax.network/v1/teleporter/messages HTTP/1.1
Host: data-api.avax.network
Accept: application/json

```

```javascript

const headers = {
  'Accept':'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key':'API_KEY'
};

fetch('https://data-api.avax.network/v1/teleporter/messages',
{
  method: 'GET',

  headers: headers
})
.then(function(res) {
    return res.json();
}).then(function(body) {
    console.log(body);
});

```

```ruby
require 'rest-client'
require 'json'

headers = {
  'Accept' => 'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key' => 'API_KEY'
}

result = RestClient.get 'https://data-api.avax.network/v1/teleporter/messages',
  params: {
  }, headers: headers

p JSON.parse(result)

```

```python

headers = {
  'Accept': 'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key': 'API_KEY'
}

r = requests.get('https://data-api.avax.network/v1/teleporter/messages', headers = headers)

print(r.json())

```

```php
<?php

require 'vendor/autoload.php';

$headers = array(
    'Accept' => 'application/json',
    'x-glacier-api-key' => 'API_KEY',
);

$client = new \GuzzleHttp\Client();

// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();

try {
    $response = $client->request('GET','https://data-api.avax.network/v1/teleporter/messages', array(
        'headers' => $headers,
        'json' => $request_body,
       )
    );
    print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
 }
 catch (\GuzzleHttp\Exception\BadResponseException $e) {
    // handle exception or api errors.
    print_r($e->getMessage());
 }

 // ...

```

```java
URL obj = new URL("https://data-api.avax.network/v1/teleporter/messages");
HttpURLConnection con = (HttpURLConnection) obj.openConnection();
con.setRequestMethod("GET");
int responseCode = con.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(
    new InputStreamReader(con.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
    response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

```

```go
       "bytes"

)

    headers := map[string][]string{

        "x-glacier-api-key": []string{"API_KEY"},
    }

    data := bytes.NewBuffer([]byte{jsonReq})
    req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", "https://data-api.avax.network/v1/teleporter/messages", data)
    req.Header = headers

    client := &http.Client{}
    resp, err := client.Do(req)
    // ...
}

```

`GET /v1/teleporter/messages`

**[Deprecated]** Lists teleporter messages. Ordered by timestamp in  descending order.

⚠️ **This operation will be removed in a future release.  Please use /v1/icm/messages endpoint instead** .

<h3 id="listteleportermessages-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 100.|
|sourceBlockchainId|query|string|false|The base58 encoded blockchain ID or evm chain ID of the source chain that the teleporter message was sent from.|
|destinationBlockchainId|query|string|false|The base58 encoded blockchain ID or evm chain ID of the destination chain that the teleporter message was sent to.|
|blockchainId|query|string|false|The base58 encoded blockchain ID of either source or destination chain that one teleporter message interacts with.|
|to|query|string|false|The address of the recipient of the teleporter message.|
|from|query|string|false|The address of the sender of the teleporter message.|
|network|query|[Network](#schemanetwork)|false|Either mainnet or testnet/fuji.|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|network|mainnet|
|network|fuji|
|network|testnet|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "nextPageToken": "string",
  "messages": [
    {
      "messageId": "string",
      "teleporterContractAddress": "string",
      "sourceBlockchainId": "string",
      "destinationBlockchainId": "string",
      "sourceEvmChainId": "string",
      "destinationEvmChainId": "string",
      "messageNonce": "string",
      "from": "string",
      "to": "string",
      "data": "string",
      "messageExecuted": true,
      "receipts": [
        {
          "receivedMessageNonce": "string",
          "relayerRewardAddress": "string"
        }
      ],
      "receiptDelivered": true,
      "rewardDetails": {
        "address": "0x71C7656EC7ab88b098defB751B7401B5f6d8976F",
        "name": "Wrapped AVAX",
        "symbol": "WAVAX",
        "decimals": 18,
        "logoUri": "https://images.ctfassets.net/gcj8jwzm6086/5VHupNKwnDYJvqMENeV7iJ/fdd6326b7a82c8388e4ee9d4be7062d4/avalanche-avax-logo.svg",
        "ercType": "ERC-20",
        "price": {
          "currencyCode": "usd",
          "value": "42.42"
        },
        "value": "string"
      },
      "sourceTransaction": {
        "txHash": "string",
        "timestamp": 0,
        "gasSpent": "string"
      },
      "status": "pending"
    }
  ]
}
```

<h3 id="listteleportermessages-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[ListTeleporterMessagesResponse](#schemalistteleportermessagesresponse)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|

    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

`GET /v1/icm/messages/{messageId}`

*Get an ICM message*

Gets an ICM message by teleporter message ID.

<h3 id="geticmmessage-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|messageId|path|string|true|The teleporter message ID of the ICM message.|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "messageId": "string",
  "icmContractAddress": "string",
  "sourceBlockchainId": "string",
  "destinationBlockchainId": "string",
  "sourceEvmChainId": "string",
  "destinationEvmChainId": "string",
  "messageNonce": "string",
  "from": "string",
  "to": "string",
  "data": "string",
  "messageExecuted": true,
  "receipts": [
    {
      "receivedMessageNonce": "string",
      "relayerRewardAddress": "string"
    }
  ],
  "receiptDelivered": true,
  "rewardDetails": {
    "address": "0x71C7656EC7ab88b098defB751B7401B5f6d8976F",
    "name": "Wrapped AVAX",
    "symbol": "WAVAX",
    "decimals": 18,
    "logoUri": "https://images.ctfassets.net/gcj8jwzm6086/5VHupNKwnDYJvqMENeV7iJ/fdd6326b7a82c8388e4ee9d4be7062d4/avalanche-avax-logo.svg",
    "ercType": "ERC-20",
    "price": {
      "currencyCode": "usd",
      "value": "42.42"
    },
    "value": "string"
  },
  "sourceTransaction": {
    "txHash": "string",
    "timestamp": 0,
    "gasSpent": "string"
  },
  "status": "pending"
}
```

<h3 id="geticmmessage-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|Inline|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<h3 id="geticmmessage-responseschema">Response Schema</h3>

#### Enumerated Values

|Property|Value|
|---|---|
|ercType|ERC-20|
|currencyCode|usd|
|currencyCode|eur|
|currencyCode|aud|
|currencyCode|cad|
|currencyCode|chf|
|currencyCode|clp|
|currencyCode|cny|
|currencyCode|czk|
|currencyCode|dkk|
|currencyCode|gbp|
|currencyCode|hkd|
|currencyCode|huf|
|currencyCode|jpy|
|currencyCode|nzd|
|status|pending|
|status|delivered|
|status|delivered_source_not_indexed|

<aside class="warning">
To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None
</aside>

## listIcmMessages

<a id="opIdlistIcmMessages"></a>

> Code samples

```shell
# You can also use wget
curl -X GET https://data-api.avax.network/v1/icm/messages \
  -H 'Accept: application/json' \
  -H 'x-glacier-api-key: API_KEY'

```

```http
GET https://data-api.avax.network/v1/icm/messages HTTP/1.1
Host: data-api.avax.network
Accept: application/json

```

```javascript

const headers = {
  'Accept':'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key':'API_KEY'
};

fetch('https://data-api.avax.network/v1/icm/messages',
{
  method: 'GET',

  headers: headers
})
.then(function(res) {
    return res.json();
}).then(function(body) {
    console.log(body);
});

```

```ruby

  'Accept' => 'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key' => 'API_KEY'
}

result = RestClient.get 'https://data-api.avax.network/v1/icm/messages',

  }, headers: headers

p JSON.parse(result)
```

```python

headers = {
  'Accept': 'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key': 'API_KEY'
}

r = requests.get('https://data-api.avax.network/v1/icm/messages', headers = headers)

print(r.json())
```

```php
<?php

require 'vendor/autoload.php';

$headers = array(
    'Accept' => 'application/json',
    'x-glacier-api-key' => 'API_KEY',
);

$client = new \GuzzleHttp\Client();

// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();

try {
    $response = $client->request('GET','https://data-api.avax.network/v1/icm/messages', array(
        'headers' => $headers,
        'json' => $request_body,
       )
    );
    print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
 }
 catch (\GuzzleHttp\Exception\BadResponseException $e) {
    // handle exception or api errors.
    print_r($e->getMessage());
 }

 // ...

```

```java
URL obj = new URL("https://data-api.avax.network/v1/icm/messages");
HttpURLConnection con = (HttpURLConnection) obj.openConnection();
con.setRequestMethod("GET");
int responseCode = con.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(
    new InputStreamReader(con.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
    response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

```

```go
package main

import (
       "bytes"
       "net/http"
)

func main() {

    headers := map[string][]string{
        "Accept": []string{"application/json"},
        "x-glacier-api-key": []string{"API_KEY"},
    }

    data := bytes.NewBuffer([]byte{jsonReq})
    req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", "https://data-api.avax.network/v1/icm/messages", data)
    req.Header = headers

    client := &http.Client{}
    resp, err := client.Do(req)
    // ...
}

```

`GET /v1/icm/messages`

*List ICM messages*

Lists ICM messages. Ordered by timestamp in descending order.

<h3 id="listicmmessages-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 100.|
|sourceBlockchainId|query|string|false|The base58 encoded blockchain ID or evm chain ID of the source chain that the ICM message was sent from.|
|destinationBlockchainId|query|string|false|The base58 encoded blockchain ID or evm chain ID of the destination chain that the ICM message was sent to.|
|blockchainId|query|string|false|The base58 encoded blockchain ID of either source or destination chain that one ICM message interacts with.|
|to|query|string|false|The address of the recipient of the ICM message.|
|from|query|string|false|The address of the sender of the ICM message.|
|network|query|[Network](#schemanetwork)|false|Either mainnet or testnet/fuji.|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|network|mainnet|
|network|fuji|
|network|testnet|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "nextPageToken": "string",
  "messages": [
    {
      "messageId": "string",
      "icmContractAddress": "string",
      "sourceBlockchainId": "string",
      "destinationBlockchainId": "string",
      "sourceEvmChainId": "string",
      "destinationEvmChainId": "string",
      "messageNonce": "string",
      "from": "string",
      "to": "string",
      "data": "string",
      "messageExecuted": true,
      "receipts": [
        {
          "receivedMessageNonce": "string",
          "relayerRewardAddress": "string"
        }
      ],
      "receiptDelivered": true,
      "rewardDetails": {
        "address": "0x71C7656EC7ab88b098defB751B7401B5f6d8976F",
        "name": "Wrapped AVAX",
        "symbol": "WAVAX",
        "decimals": 18,
        "logoUri": "https://images.ctfassets.net/gcj8jwzm6086/5VHupNKwnDYJvqMENeV7iJ/fdd6326b7a82c8388e4ee9d4be7062d4/avalanche-avax-logo.svg",
        "ercType": "ERC-20",
        "price": {
          "currencyCode": "usd",
          "value": "42.42"
        },
        "value": "string"
      },
      "sourceTransaction": {
        "txHash": "string",
        "timestamp": 0,
        "gasSpent": "string"
      },
      "status": "pending"
    }
  ]
}
```

<h3 id="listicmmessages-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[ListIcmMessagesResponse](#schemalisticmmessagesresponse)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

`GET /v1/icm/addresses/{address}/messages`

*List ICM messages by address*

Lists ICM messages by address. Ordered by timestamp in descending order.

<h3 id="listicmmessagesbyaddress-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 100.|
|address|path|string|true|An EVM address.|
|network|query|[Network](#schemanetwork)|false|Either mainnet or testnet/fuji.|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|network|mainnet|
|network|fuji|
|network|testnet|

<h1 id="data-api-webhooks">Webhooks</h1>

Manage webhooks and get notified for various onchain activities.

## createWebhook

<a id="opIdcreateWebhook"></a>

> Code samples

```shell
# You can also use wget
curl -X POST https://data-api.avax.network/v1/webhooks \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Accept: application/json' \
  -H 'x-glacier-api-key: API_KEY'

```

```http
POST https://data-api.avax.network/v1/webhooks HTTP/1.1
Host: data-api.avax.network
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

```

```javascript
const inputBody = '{
  "eventType": "address_activity",
  "url": "string",
  "name": "string",
  "description": "string",
  "chainId": "string",
  "metadata": {
    "eventSignatures": [
      "0x61cbb2a3dee0b6064c2e681aadd61677fb4ef319f0b547508d495626f5a62f64"
    ],
    "addresses": [
      "0xB97EF9Ef8734C71904D8002F8b6Bc66Dd9c48a6E"
    ]
  },
  "includeInternalTxs": true,
  "includeLogs": true
}';
const headers = {
  'Content-Type':'application/json',
  'Accept':'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key':'API_KEY'
};

fetch('https://data-api.avax.network/v1/webhooks',
{
  method: 'POST',
  body: inputBody,
  headers: headers
})
.then(function(res) {
    return res.json();
}).then(function(body) {
    console.log(body);
});

```

`POST /v1/webhooks`

*Create a webhook*

Create a new webhook.

> Body parameter

```json
{
  "eventType": "address_activity",
  "url": "string",
  "name": "string",
  "description": "string",
  "chainId": "string",
  "metadata": {
    "eventSignatures": [
      "0x61cbb2a3dee0b6064c2e681aadd61677fb4ef319f0b547508d495626f5a62f64"
    ],
    "addresses": [
      "0xB97EF9Ef8734C71904D8002F8b6Bc66Dd9c48a6E"
    ]
  },
  "includeInternalTxs": true,
  "includeLogs": true
}
```

<h3 id="createwebhook-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|body|body|any|true|none|

> Example responses

> 201 Response

```json
{
  "id": "string",
  "url": "string",
  "chainId": "string",
  "status": "active",
  "createdAt": 0,
  "name": "string",
  "description": "string",
  "eventType": "address_activity",
  "metadata": {
    "eventSignatures": [
      "0x61cbb2a3dee0b6064c2e681aadd61677fb4ef319f0b547508d495626f5a62f64"
    ],
    "addresses": [
      "0xB97EF9Ef8734C71904D8002F8b6Bc66Dd9c48a6E"
    ]
  },
  "includeInternalTxs": true,
  "includeLogs": true
}
```

<h3 id="createwebhook-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|201|[Created](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.2)|Successful response|Inline|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<h3 id="createwebhook-responseschema">Response Schema</h3>

#### Enumerated Values

|Property|Value|
|---|---|
|status|active|
|status|inactive|
|eventType|address_activity|
|status|active|
|status|inactive|
|eventType|primary_network_address_activity|
|keyType|addresses|
|balanceType|unlockedUnstaked|
|balanceType|unlockedStaked|

## listWebhooks

<a id="opIdlistWebhooks"></a>

> Code samples

```shell
# You can also use wget
curl -X GET https://data-api.avax.network/v1/webhooks \
  -H 'Accept: application/json' \
  -H 'x-glacier-api-key: API_KEY'

```

```http
GET https://data-api.avax.network/v1/webhooks HTTP/1.1
Host: data-api.avax.network
Accept: application/json

```

```javascript

const headers = {
  'Accept':'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key':'API_KEY'
};

fetch('https://data-api.avax.network/v1/webhooks',
{
  method: 'GET',

  headers: headers
})
.then(function(res) {
    return res.json();
}).then(function(body) {
    console.log(body);
});

```

```ruby
require 'rest-client'
require 'json'

headers = {
  'Accept' => 'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key' => 'API_KEY'
}

result = RestClient.get 'https://data-api.avax.network/v1/webhooks',
  params: {
  }, headers: headers

p JSON.parse(result)

```

```python
import requests
headers = {
  'Accept': 'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key': 'API_KEY'
}

r = requests.get('https://data-api.avax.network/v1/webhooks', headers = headers)

print(r.json())

```

```php
<?php

require 'vendor/autoload.php';

$headers = array(
    'Accept' => 'application/json',
    'x-glacier-api-key' => 'API_KEY',
);

$client = new \GuzzleHttp\Client();

// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();

try {
    $response = $client->request('GET','https://data-api.avax.network/v1/webhooks', array(
        'headers' => $headers,
        'json' => $request_body,
       )
    );
    print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
 }
 catch (\GuzzleHttp\Exception\BadResponseException $e) {
    // handle exception or api errors.
    print_r($e->getMessage());
 }

 // ...

```

```java
URL obj = new URL("https://data-api.avax.network/v1/webhooks");
HttpURLConnection con = (HttpURLConnection) obj.openConnection();
con.setRequestMethod("GET");
int responseCode = con.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(
    new InputStreamReader(con.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
    response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

```

```go
package main

import (
       "bytes"
       "net/http"
)

func main() {

    headers := map[string][]string{
        "Accept": []string{"application/json"},
        "x-glacier-api-key": []string{"API_KEY"},
    }

    data := bytes.NewBuffer([]byte{jsonReq})
    req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", "https://data-api.avax.network/v1/webhooks", data)
    req.Header = headers

    client := &http.Client{}
    resp, err := client.Do(req)
    // ...
}

```

`GET /v1/webhooks`

*List webhooks*

Lists webhooks for the user.

<h3 id="listwebhooks-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 100.|
|status|query|[WebhookStatus](#schemawebhookstatus)|false|Status of the webhook. Use "active" to return only active webhooks, "inactive" to return only inactive webhooks. Else if no status is provided, all configured webhooks will be returned.|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|status|active|
|status|inactive|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "nextPageToken": "string",
  "webhooks": [
    {
      "id": "string",
      "url": "string",
      "chainId": "string",
      "status": "active",
      "createdAt": 0,
      "name": "string",
      "description": "string",
      "eventType": "address_activity",
      "metadata": {
        "eventSignatures": [
          "0x61cbb2a3dee0b6064c2e681aadd61677fb4ef319f0b547508d495626f5a62f64"
        ],
        "addresses": [
          "0xB97EF9Ef8734C71904D8002F8b6Bc66Dd9c48a6E"
        ]
      },
      "includeInternalTxs": true,
      "includeLogs": true
    }
  ]
}
```

<h3 id="listwebhooks-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[ListWebhooksResponse](#schemalistwebhooksresponse)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

`PATCH /v1/webhooks/{id}`

*Update a webhook*

Updates an existing webhook.

> Body parameter

```json
{
  "name": "string",
  "description": "string",
  "url": "string",
  "status": "active",
  "includeInternalTxs": true,
  "includeLogs": true
}
```

<h3 id="updatewebhook-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|id|path|string|true|The webhook identifier.|
|body|body|[UpdateWebhookRequest](#schemaupdatewebhookrequest)|true|none|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "id": "string",
  "url": "string",
  "chainId": "string",
  "status": "active",
  "createdAt": 0,
  "name": "string",
  "description": "string",
  "eventType": "address_activity",
  "metadata": {
    "eventSignatures": [
      "0x61cbb2a3dee0b6064c2e681aadd61677fb4ef319f0b547508d495626f5a62f64"
    ],
    "addresses": [
      "0xB97EF9Ef8734C71904D8002F8b6Bc66Dd9c48a6E"
    ]
  },
  "includeInternalTxs": true,
  "includeLogs": true
}
```

<h3 id="updatewebhook-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|Inline|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<h3 id="updatewebhook-responseschema">Response Schema</h3>

|Property|Value|
|---|---|
|status|active|
|status|inactive|
|eventType|address_activity|

|status|inactive|

`PATCH /v1/webhooks/{id}/addresses`

*Add addresses to EVM activity webhook*

Add addresses to webhook. Only valid for EVM activity webhooks.

> Body parameter

```json
{
  "addresses": [
    "0xB97EF9Ef8734C71904D8002F8b6Bc66Dd9c48a6E"
  ]
}
```

<h3 id="addaddressestowebhook-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|id|path|string|true|The webhook identifier.|
|body|body|[AddressesChangeRequest](#schemaaddresseschangerequest)|true|none|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "id": "string",
  "url": "string",
  "chainId": "string",
  "status": "active",
  "createdAt": 0,
  "name": "string",
  "description": "string",
  "eventType": "address_activity",
  "metadata": {
    "eventSignatures": [
      "0x61cbb2a3dee0b6064c2e681aadd61677fb4ef319f0b547508d495626f5a62f64"
    ],
    "addresses": [
      "0xB97EF9Ef8734C71904D8002F8b6Bc66Dd9c48a6E"
    ]
  },
  "includeInternalTxs": true,
  "includeLogs": true
}
```

<h3 id="addaddressestowebhook-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[EVMAddressActivityResponse](#schemaevmaddressactivityresponse)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<aside class="warning">
To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None
</aside>

`DELETE /v1/webhooks/{id}/addresses`

*Remove addresses from EVM activity  webhook*

Remove addresses from webhook. Only valid for EVM activity webhooks.

```json
{
  "addresses": [
    "0xB97EF9Ef8734C71904D8002F8b6Bc66Dd9c48a6E"
  ]
}
```

`GET /v1/webhooks/{id}/addresses`

*List adresses by EVM activity webhooks*

List adresses by webhook. Only valid for EVM activity webhooks.

<h3 id="getaddressesfromwebhook-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return. The minimum page size is 1. The maximum pageSize is 100.|
|id|path|string|true|The webhook identifier.|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "nextPageToken": "string",
  "addresses": [
    "string"
  ],
  "totalAddresses": 0
}
```

`GET /v1/apiUsageMetrics`

*Get usage metrics for the Data API*

Gets metrics for Data API usage over a specified time interval aggregated at the specified time-duration granularity.

<h3 id="getapiusagemetrics-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|orgId|query|string|false|Organization ID to fetch usage metrics for|
|startTimestamp|query|integer|false|The start time of the range as a UNIX timestamp. The requested start time  will be rounded down to 0:00 UTC of the day.|
|endTimestamp|query|integer|false|The end time of the range as a UNIX timestamp. The requested end time will be rounded down to 0:00 UTC of the day.|
|timeInterval|query|[TimeIntervalGranularityExtended](#schematimeintervalgranularityextended)|false|Time interval granularity for data aggregation|
|groupBy|query|[UsageMetricsGroupByEnum](#schemausagemetricsgroupbyenum)|false|Query param for the criterion used for grouping metrics|
|chainId|query|string|false|Filter data by chain ID.|
|responseCode|query|string|false|Filter data by response status code.|
|requestType|query|string|false|Filter data by request type.|
|apiKeyId|query|string|false|Filter data by API key ID.|
|requestPath|query|string|false|Filter data by request path.|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|timeInterval|minute|
|timeInterval|hourly|
|timeInterval|daily|
|timeInterval|weekly|
|timeInterval|monthly|
|groupBy|requestPath|
|groupBy|responseCode|
|groupBy|chainId|
|groupBy|apiKeyId|
|groupBy|requestType|
|requestType|data|
|requestType|rpc|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "aggregateDuration": "string",
  "orgId": "string",
  "metrics": [
    {
      "timestamp": 0,
      "values": [
        {
          "groupedBy": "requestPath",
          "groupValue": "string",
          "totalRequests": 0,
          "requestsPerSecond": 0,
          "successRatePercent": 0,
          "medianResponseTimeMsecs": 0,
          "invalidRequests": 0,
          "apiCreditsUsed": 0,
          "apiCreditsWasted": 0
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

<h3 id="getapiusagemetrics-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[UsageMetricsResponseDTO](#schemausagemetricsresponsedto)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

```javascript
  'Accept':'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key':'API_KEY'
};

fetch('https://data-api.avax.network/v1/apiLogs',
{
  method: 'GET',

  headers: headers
})
.then(function(res) {
    return res.json();
}).then(function(body) {
    console.log(body);
});

```

```ruby
require 'rest-client'
require 'json'

headers = {
  'Accept' => 'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key' => 'API_KEY'
}

result = RestClient.get 'https://data-api.avax.network/v1/apiLogs',
  params: {
  }, headers: headers

p JSON.parse(result)

```

```python

headers = {
  'Accept': 'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key': 'API_KEY'
}

r = requests.get('https://data-api.avax.network/v1/apiLogs', headers = headers)

print(r.json())

```

```php
<?php

require 'vendor/autoload.php';

$headers = array(
    'Accept' => 'application/json',
    'x-glacier-api-key' => 'API_KEY',
);

$client = new \GuzzleHttp\Client();

// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();

try {
    $response = $client->request('GET','https://data-api.avax.network/v1/apiLogs', array(
        'headers' => $headers,
        'json' => $request_body,
       )
    );
    print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
 }
 catch (\GuzzleHttp\Exception\BadResponseException $e) {
    // handle exception or api errors.
    print_r($e->getMessage());
 }

 // ...

```

```java
URL obj = new URL("https://data-api.avax.network/v1/apiLogs");
HttpURLConnection con = (HttpURLConnection) obj.openConnection();
con.setRequestMethod("GET");
int responseCode = con.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(
    new InputStreamReader(con.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
    response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

```

```go
package main

import (
       "bytes"
       "net/http"
)

func main() {

    headers := map[string][]string{
        "Accept": []string{"application/json"},
        "x-glacier-api-key": []string{"API_KEY"},
    }

    data := bytes.NewBuffer([]byte{jsonReq})
    req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", "https://data-api.avax.network/v1/apiLogs", data)
    req.Header = headers

    client := &http.Client{}
    resp, err := client.Do(req)
    // ...
}

```

`GET /v1/apiLogs`

*Get logs for requests made by client*

Gets logs for requests made by client over a specified time interval for a specific organization.

<h3 id="getapilogs-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|orgId|query|string|false|Organization ID to fetch usage metrics for|
|startTimestamp|query|integer|false|The start time of the range as a UNIX timestamp. The requested start time  will be rounded down to 0:00 UTC of the day.|
|endTimestamp|query|integer|false|The end time of the range as a UNIX timestamp. The requested end time will be rounded down to 0:00 UTC of the day.|
|chainId|query|string|false|Filter data by chain ID.|
|responseCode|query|string|false|Filter data by response status code.|
|requestType|query|string|false|Filter data by request type.|
|apiKeyId|query|string|false|Filter data by API key ID.|
|requestPath|query|string|false|Filter data by request path.|
|pageToken|query|string|false|A page token, received from a previous list call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.|
|pageSize|query|integer|false|The maximum number of items to return.|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|requestType|data|
|requestType|rpc|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "nextPageToken": "string",
  "orgId": "string",
  "logs": [
    {
      "orgId": "string",
      "logId": "string",
      "eventTimestamp": 0,
      "apiKeyId": "string",
      "apiKeyAlias": "string",
      "hostRegion": "string",
      "requestType": "data",
      "requestPath": "string",
      "apiCreditsConsumed": 0,
      "requestDurationMsecs": 0,
      "responseCode": 0,
      "chainId": "string",
      "rpcMethod": "string",
      "metadata": {
        "ipAddress": "string",
        "host": "string",
        "userAgent": "string"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

<h3 id="getapilogs-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[LogsResponseDTO](#schemalogsresponsedto)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<aside class="warning">
To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None
</aside>

`GET /v1/subnetRpcUsageMetrics`

*Get usage metrics for the Subnet RPC*

Gets metrics for public Subnet RPC usage over a specified time interval aggregated at the specified time-duration granularity.

<h3 id="getsubnetrpcusagemetrics-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|timeInterval|query|[SubnetRpcTimeIntervalGranularity](#schemasubnetrpctimeintervalgranularity)|false|Time interval granularity for data aggregation for subnet|
|startTimestamp|query|integer|false|The start time of the range as a UNIX timestamp. The requested start time  will be rounded down to 0:00 UTC of the day.|
|endTimestamp|query|integer|false|The end time of the range as a UNIX timestamp. The requested end time will be rounded down to 0:00 UTC of the day.|
|groupBy|query|[RpcUsageMetricsGroupByEnum](#schemarpcusagemetricsgroupbyenum)|false|Query param for the criterion used for grouping metrics|
|chainId|query|string|false|Filter data by chain ID.|
|responseCode|query|string|false|Filter data by response status code.|
|rpcMethod|query|string|false|Filter data by RPC method.|
|rlBypassApiToken|query|string|false|Filter data by Rl Bypass API Token.|

#### Detailed descriptions

**timeInterval**: Time interval granularity for data aggregation for subnet
     rpc metrics

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|timeInterval|hourly|
|timeInterval|daily|
|timeInterval|weekly|
|timeInterval|monthly|
|groupBy|rpcMethod|
|groupBy|responseCode|
|groupBy|rlBypassToken|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "aggregateDuration": "string",
  "metrics": [
    {
      "timestamp": 0,
      "values": [
        {
          "totalRequests": 0,
          "apiCreditsUsed": 0,
          "requestsPerSecond": 0,
          "successRatePercent": 0,
          "medianResponseTimeMsecs": 0,
          "invalidRequests": 0,
          "apiCreditsWasted": 0,
          "groupedBy": "rpcMethod",
          "groupValue": "string"
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "chainId": "string"
}
```

<h3 id="getsubnetrpcusagemetrics-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[SubnetRpcUsageMetricsResponseDTO](#schemasubnetrpcusagemetricsresponsedto)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None

`GET /v1/rpcUsageMetrics`

**[Deprecated]**  Gets metrics for public Subnet RPC usage over a specified time interval aggregated at the specified time-duration granularity.

⚠️ **This operation will be removed in a future release.  Please use /v1/subnetRpcUsageMetrics endpoint instead**.

<h3 id="getrpcusagemetrics-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|timeInterval|query|[SubnetRpcTimeIntervalGranularity](#schemasubnetrpctimeintervalgranularity)|false|Time interval granularity for data aggregation for subnet|
|startTimestamp|query|integer|false|The start time of the range as a UNIX timestamp. The requested start time  will be rounded down to 0:00 UTC of the day.|
|endTimestamp|query|integer|false|The end time of the range as a UNIX timestamp. The requested end time will be rounded down to 0:00 UTC of the day.|
|groupBy|query|[RpcUsageMetricsGroupByEnum](#schemarpcusagemetricsgroupbyenum)|false|Query param for the criterion used for grouping metrics|
|chainId|query|string|false|Filter data by chain ID.|
|responseCode|query|string|false|Filter data by response status code.|
|rpcMethod|query|string|false|Filter data by RPC method.|
|rlBypassApiToken|query|string|false|Filter data by Rl Bypass API Token.|

#### Detailed descriptions

**timeInterval**: Time interval granularity for data aggregation for subnet
     rpc metrics

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|timeInterval|hourly|
|timeInterval|daily|
|timeInterval|weekly|
|timeInterval|monthly|
|groupBy|rpcMethod|
|groupBy|responseCode|
|groupBy|rlBypassToken|

```json
          "requestsPerSecond": 0,
          "successRatePercent": 0,
          "medianResponseTimeMsecs": 0,
```

```go
)

func main() {

    headers := map[string][]string{
        "Accept": []string{"application/json"},
        "x-glacier-api-key": []string{"API_KEY"},
    }

    data := bytes.NewBuffer([]byte{jsonReq})
    req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", "https://data-api.avax.network/v1/primaryNetworkRpcUsageMetrics", data)
    req.Header = headers

    client := &http.Client{}
    resp, err := client.Do(req)
    // ...
}

```

`GET /v1/primaryNetworkRpcUsageMetrics`

*Get usage metrics for the Primary Network RPC*

Gets metrics for public Primary Network RPC usage over  a specified time interval aggregated at the specified  time-duration granularity.

<h3 id="getprimarynetworkrpcusagemetrics-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|timeInterval|query|[PrimaryNetworkRpcTimeIntervalGranularity](#schemaprimarynetworkrpctimeintervalgranularity)|false|Time interval granularity for data aggregation for subnet|
|startTimestamp|query|integer|false|The start time of the range as a UNIX timestamp. The requested start time  will be rounded down to 0:00 UTC of the day.|
|endTimestamp|query|integer|false|The end time of the range as a UNIX timestamp. The requested end time will be rounded down to 0:00 UTC of the day.|
|groupBy|query|[PrimaryNetworkRpcMetricsGroupByEnum](#schemaprimarynetworkrpcmetricsgroupbyenum)|false|Query param for the criterion used for grouping metrics|
|responseCode|query|string|false|Filter data by response status code.|
|requestPath|query|string|false|Filter data by request path.|
|country|query|string|false|Filter data by Country. Input must be a valid ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code. "XX" - Represents clients without country code data. "T1" - Represents clients using the Tor network.|
|continent|query|string|false|Filter data by Continent. Input must be a valid ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 continent code.|
|userAgent|query|string|false|Filter data by User Agent.|
|rpcMethod|query|string|false|Filter data by RPC method.|
|network|query|[Network](#schemanetwork)|true|Either mainnet or testnet/fuji.|

#### Detailed descriptions

**timeInterval**: Time interval granularity for data aggregation for subnet
     rpc metrics

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|timeInterval|hourly|
|timeInterval|daily|
|timeInterval|weekly|
|timeInterval|monthly|
|groupBy|rpcMethod|
|groupBy|requestPath|
|groupBy|responseCode|
|groupBy|country|
|groupBy|continent|
|groupBy|userAgent|
|network|mainnet|
|network|fuji|
|network|testnet|

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "aggregateDuration": "string",
  "metrics": [
    {
      "timestamp": 0,
      "values": [
        {
          "totalRequests": 0,
          "apiCreditsUsed": 0,
          "requestsPerSecond": 0,
          "successRatePercent": 0,
          "medianResponseTimeMsecs": 0,
          "invalidRequests": 0,
          "apiCreditsWasted": 0,
          "groupedBy": "rpcMethod",
          "groupValue": "string"
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "network": "mainnet"
}
```

<h3 id="getprimarynetworkrpcusagemetrics-responses">Responses</h3>

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|
|200|[OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1)|Successful response|[PrimaryNetworkRpcUsageMetricsResponseDTO](#schemaprimarynetworkrpcusagemetricsresponsedto)|
|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require 
    authorization credentials but the client lacks proper authentication 
    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem 
    persists for a longer duration.|[InternalServerError](#schemainternalservererror)|
|502|[Bad Gateway](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.3)|This is an internal error indicating invalid response 
      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|
|503|[Service Unavailable](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.4)|The error is returned for certain routes on a particular
    Subnet. This indicates an internal problem with our Subnet node, and may 
    not necessarily mean the Subnet is down or affected.|[ServiceUnavailable](#schemaserviceunavailable)|

<aside class="warning">
To perform this operation, you must be authenticated by means of one of the following methods:
apiKey, None
</aside>

<h1 id="data-api-signature-aggregator">Signature Aggregator</h1>

Request signatures for a Warp message from Subnet validators

## aggregateSignatures

<a id="opIdaggregateSignatures"></a>

> Code samples

```shell
# You can also use wget
curl -X POST https://data-api.avax.network/v1/signatureAggregator/{network}/aggregateSignatures \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Accept: application/json' \
  -H 'x-glacier-api-key: API_KEY'

```

```http
POST https://data-api.avax.network/v1/signatureAggregator/{network}/aggregateSignatures HTTP/1.1
Host: data-api.avax.network
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

```

```javascript
const inputBody = '{
  "message": "",
  "justification": "0001abcdabcfff",
  "signingSubnetId": "7WtoAMPhrmh5KosDUsFL9yTcvw7YSxiKHPpdfs4JsgW47oZT5",
  "quorumPercentage": 50,
  "quorumPercentageBuffer": 50,
  "pChainHeight": 1155
}';
const headers = {
  'Content-Type':'application/json',
  'Accept':'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key':'API_KEY'
};

fetch('https://data-api.avax.network/v1/signatureAggregator/{network}/aggregateSignatures',
{
  method: 'POST',
  body: inputBody,
  headers: headers
})
.then(function(res) {
    return res.json();
}).then(function(body) {
    console.log(body);
});

```

```ruby
require 'rest-client'
require 'json'

headers = {
  'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
  'Accept' => 'application/json',
  'x-glacier-api-key' => 'API_KEY'
}

result = RestClient.post 'https://data-api.avax.network/v1/signatureAggregator/{network}/aggregateSignatures',
  params: {
  }, headers: headers

p JSON.parse(result)

```

`POST /v1/signatureAggregator/{network}/aggregateSignatures`

*Aggregate Signatures*

Aggregates Signatures for a Warp message from Subnet validators.

> Body parameter

```json
{
  "message": "",
  "justification": "0001abcdabcfff",
  "signingSubnetId": "7WtoAMPhrmh5KosDUsFL9yTcvw7YSxiKHPpdfs4JsgW47oZT5",
  "quorumPercentage": 50,
  "quorumPercentageBuffer": 50,
  "pChainHeight": 1155
}
```

<h3 id="aggregatesignatures-parameters">Parameters</h3>

|Name|In|Type|Required|Description|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|network|path|[Network](#schemanetwork)|true|Either mainnet or testnet/fuji.|
|body|body|[SignatureAggregatorRequest](#schemasignatureaggregatorrequest)|true|none|

#### Enumerated Values

|Parameter|Value|
|---|---|
|network|mainnet|
|network|fuji|
|network|testnet|

> Example responses

> 201 Response

```json
{
  "signedMessage": ""
}
```

|Status|Meaning|Description|Schema|
|---|---|---|---|

|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
|401|[Unauthorized](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1)|When a client attempts to access resources that require

    in the request, the server responds with 401.|[Unauthorized](#schemaunauthorized)|
|403|[Forbidden](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3)|When a client attempts to access resources with valid
    credentials but doesn't have the privilege to perform that action, 
    the server responds with 403.|[Forbidden](#schemaforbidden)|
|404|[Not Found](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.4)|The error is mostly returned when the client requests
    with either mistyped URL, or the passed resource is moved or deleted, 
    or the resource doesn't exist.|[NotFound](#schemanotfound)|
|429|[Too Many Requests](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6585#section-4)|This error is returned when the client has sent too many,
    and has hit the rate limit.|[TooManyRequests](#schematoomanyrequests)|
|500|[Internal Server Error](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.6.1)|The error is a generic server side error that is 
    returned for any uncaught and unexpected issues on the server side. 
    This should be very rare, and you may reach out to us if the problem

      received by the client-facing proxy or gateway from the upstream server.|[BadGateway](#schemabadgateway)|

`GET /v1/avax/supply`

*Get AVAX supply information*

Get AVAX supply information that includes  total supply, circulating supply, total p burned, total c burned,  total x burned, total staked, total locked, total rewards,  and last updated.

> Example responses

> 200 Response

```json
{
  "circulatingSupply": "454257251.7149865026656081965",
  "totalSupply": "454257251.7149865026656081965",
  "totalPBurned": "15039.56226661934233308491042",
  "totalCBurned": "4868917.656222642629429578983",
  "totalXBurned": "45577.89911362119281041174838",
  "totalStaked": "199605247.61112",
  "totalLocked": "36162978.31786130001530843447",
  "totalRewards": "99186786.83258938583018127214",
  "lastUpdated": "2025-11-05T20:15:15.678Z",
  "genesisUnlock": "360000000",
  "l1ValidatorFees": "6786.673898334"
}
```

|---|---|---|---|

|400|[Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1)|Bad requests generally mean the client has passed invalid 
    or malformed parameters. Error messages in the response could help in 
    evaluating the error.|[BadRequest](#schemabadrequest)|
